As SB, I have decent position postflop if called. Facing a button min-raise to 300 into a 150 pot, this is a spot to 3-bet for value and isolation. Standard sizing is around 3x the open (900 total). Pot will be 450 + 900 = 1350 if called flat, good for our equity. Minni in BB may defend wide or fold, but even if both call, KK is way ahead. Larger size risks fold equity too much preflop with deep stacks (~137BB effective). Raising to 900 balances value and fold equity while denying equity to worse hands.

Flash opened to 300 and Elon 3‑bet to 900 — a clear sign of strength or an isolation attempt. I hold Tc4d, a weak, unsuited, poorly connected hand with a bad kicker and almost always dominated by 3‑bet/open ranges. The pot is 1,300 and I must call 800 (I’d need ~38% equity after a call = 800/(1300+800)), which Tc4o won’t have multiway against these ranges. With two aggressive opponents, out of position postflop and no flush/suited playability, this is a clear fold — save chips for better spots.

I opened to 300 from the button with Ace-Deuce offsuit. Elon, in the Small Blind, has 3-bet to 900. Minni in the Big Blind has folded. My hand, A2o, is very weak against a 3-bet range from a competent player like Elon, especially since he is out of position and usually needs a stronger range to re-raise. While I am in position, calling a 3-bet with A2o often leads to difficult situations post-flop where I am dominated or unable to realize my equity. Folding is the most disciplined and long-term profitable play here to preserve my stack.

3-handed play (Claude inactive). On the button first to act preflop with 6c3c, suited connectors. Deep stacks (~140BB). This hand has good playability, straight/flush potential, position advantage. Standard to open-raise wide range from button in short-handed play. Consistent with previous opening sizes (often 300). Raise to 300 to build pot, take initiative. Folding too tight; limping poor as it doesn't apply pressure and plays out of position OOP postflop.

Button (Elon) opened to 300 in a 3-handed pot and I’m in the SB with Tc9h. This is a decent, connected off‑suit hand but I’m out of position to both the button and the big blind. Calling 250 more (300 total committed) is a small, cheap price to see a flop with good playability and implied odds given 105bb effective stacks. A min 3‑bet to 500 is thin OOP with T9o — it won’t fold out the BB reliably and can just create awkward multiway spots or face a 4‑bet. I’ll call, keep the pot manageable, and play the flop straightforwardly: continue with strong pairs or good draws, fold to significant turn/river pressure when missed, and look to exploit favorable flops postflop.

I am in the big blind with 6d 3s. The button raised to 300 and the small blind called. Even though I'm getting decent pot odds to call the additional 200, 63o is an extremely weak hand that performs poorly in multi-way pots and is difficult to play out of position. Folding is the most disciplined move here to avoid losing more chips with a marginal hand.

Flop gives me middle pair: Tc9h on Jc Kc 9s. I’m out of position and Elon opened preflop, so his range is wide but contains a lot of Kx/Jx and broadway combos that currently beat me. With 102 BB effective and a 700-chip pot I want to control the size of the pot and let him bluff/CI-bet with worse. Checking also lets me realize showdown value versus his air and calling-range — I can call reasonable cbets and re-evaluate on the turn (I have 4 Q outs to a Broadway straight and backdoor club possibilities). Plan: check here; call a standard small-to-medium c-bet (≈¼–½–pot) for pot control and fold to large polarizing raises unless I pick up on the turn or have a strong read. If he checks back I’ll look to extract value or bluff-turn depending on the next card (favoring bets when I improve to two pair or when the turn gives a favorable fold equity).

Flop board JcKc9s is extremely wet: two clubs, broadway straight draws (Q10, T8, Q8). My 6c3c gives me the nut flush draw (9 outs to nuts with Ac/Qc/Tc/8c/7c/5c/4c/2c, but precisely 9 club outs). Also backdoor straight potential with 6. Very strong speculative hand now turned monster draw (~35-40% equity vs most calling ranges). Minni called preflop raise OOP from SB (loose range: pairs, suited connectors, broadways, Ax). Her check suggests weakness (no top pair/value or trapping, unlikely). Position advantage on button. Deep stacks (~140BB/100BB). Pot 700. Betting as semi-bluff maximizes EV: fold equity vs weak hands/draws, value from worse draws/medium pairs, build pot for when we hit. Checking risks giving free card to her draws/improves. Size ~half-pot (350-400) balances range, denies equity to her floats/draws like Q10s, 87s, sets unlikely on this board. Consistent with aggressive image from history (frequent PF raises, postflop aggression). Plan: bet 400, fold to raise (unless tiny), c/c turn if misses, barrel if improves/checks behind rivers sometimes.

I have Tc9h — middle pair (pair of 9s) on Jc Kc 9s. Elon’s 400 into a 1100 pot is a small/medium c‑bet from a wide BTN range that contains many hands I currently beat (floats, missed broadways) but also a lot that beat me (Kx, Jx). Calling is the best play: I keep the pot controlled out of position, realize showdown value, and retain the 4 Queens as clean outs to a Broadway straight plus additional outs to make two‑pair/trips (tens/remaining nines). I won’t thin‑value raise OOP with a marginal hand against a likely stronger range. Plan: call 400 now; re‑evaluate on the turn — fold to large polarizing raises unless I’ve improved to two‑pair/trips/straight, and extract value or apply pressure only when I reliably beat his range.

Board is Jc Kc 9s 7d, I hold Tc 9h — a single pair (nines) OOP with a 10 kicker. The 7d is a blank relative to the flop: it also kills the runner-runner club possibility so my only clean river improvements are 11 outs (3 tens, 2 nines, 3 kings, 3 jacks) ≈ 24% to make two‑pair/trips. Elon opened BTN and c‑bet a small 400 on the flop, which is consistent with a wide continuing range (Kx/Jx, broadways, floats and some bluffs). Out of position with a marginal made hand, pot control and showdown value are my priorities — I don’t want to inflate the pot when I’m often behind to his value hands and have limited fold equity. I’ll check the turn to keep the pot manageable, realize equity, and call reasonable river bets from bluffs/misses; I’ll fold to large polarizing barrels unless I improve or have a specific read. If Elon checks back I’ll take my showdown.

Turn board 7d is a complete blank for my nut club flush draw (still 9 clean outs, ~19% to hit river). No improvement, but position on button vs Minni in SB who has checked twice (flop call of 400 into 1100 pot suggests medium strength: likely Jx/Kx/9x, weaker pairs like 88/TT, stubborn draws like Q10o/s, Q8s, or floats; unlikely sets/two-pair given check-call line OOP). Her double-check indicates weakness or trapping, but history shows Minni mixes aggression/passivity. Pot 1500, SPR ~6.5 effective with her 9850. Betting thin value semi-bluff exploits: high fold equity vs missed draws/floats (Q10, 87s, club draws whiffed), value from worse like underpairs/JT; denies her equity on rivers. Checking risks free card to her improves (e.g., any club, 10/8/Q) and smaller pot if I hit. Size ~half-pot (750) balances with my value range (KJ+, sets) and other bluffs (missed suited connectors); consistent with flop aggression and image. Fold to raise (pot committed light), c/c/call river if misses/brick unless scary.

I have Tc9h on Jc Kc 9s 7d — middle pair (nines) out of position. Elon opened BTN, c‑bet the flop small (400) and fired a modest turn (750). Folding here would be too weak given my showdown value vs missed broadways/floats; raising OOP with a marginal pair is thin and just bloats the pot against a range that contains lots of Kx/Jx that currently dominate me. Pot is 2250 and the call is 750, so I need ~25% equity to justify a call. Raw river outs to improve (3 tens, 2 nines, 4 queens to complete the 9‑10‑J‑Q‑K straight, plus board‑pairing J/K outs) give me plenty of equity (roughly mid‑20s to low‑30s% even after discounting some “dirty” outs that help his range). Call to keep the pot manageable, realize equity, and re‑evaluate on the river — fold to large polarizing river jams unless I improve to two‑pair/trips/straight, call smaller river bets, and extract value when I do hit.

River (Jc Kc 9s 7d 4s): I have Tc9h — one pair (nines) with a 10 kicker. Elon opened BTN and barreled flop (400) and turn (750) — his continuing range is weighted toward Kx/Jx and strong broadways that currently beat me, but it also contains floats and bluffs that I beat. Pot is 3000 and I’m OOP; betting here has very little fold equity versus his likely value-heavy range and mostly bloats the pot when I’m often behind. Checking preserves showdown equity, keeps the pot under control, and forces him to either bluff or show down. I’ll check to take my marginal hand to showdown rather than thin-value/turn it into a costly bluff attempt.

River 4s is a complete blank - no flush completes (spades only 9s4s, clubs still possible but not hit), no new straights, low card. I have pure air: 3c6c missed nut club flush draw entirely, no pair/draw left (~0% equity). However, my button raise + double barrel on wet flop (JcKc9s heavy with clubs/straight draws) + turn barrel reps very strong: top pair top kicker (KJ/KQ/AK), sets (JJ/KK/99), two-pair combos, made clubs (though board whiffed clubs). Minni's OOP check-call pre/flop/turn line (invested ~1450 into 3000 pot) is capped: likely Kx (KQ/KT/K9s), Jx (AJ/JT), 9x, medium pairs (TT/88), stubborn Q10s/108s/Q8s draws that missed/whiffed turn/river. Unlikely nuts given double check OOP (trapping rare for her). Her river check screams weakness/medium showdown. Bluff EV+ here: high fold equity (~60%+) vs her capped range (folds worse pairs/draws to 3rd barrel), no showdown value anyway. Position, low SPR (~3 for her), aggressive table image (prior barrels, history of PF opens/cbets). Size 2/3 pot (2000) maximizes fold equity while looking value-ish (e.g., KT/K9/JT), pot-commits light, <half her stack so callable by J9+ but she folds those. Check-behind loses 3000 uncontested (negative EV given FE), bluff exploits her history of folding to aggression (e.g., Hand 1 river check-fold implied, Hand 21 river fold). Plan executed: barrel river as semi-bluff continuation even on miss.

Board: Jc Kc 9s 7d 4s — I have Tc9h (one pair, nines, 10 kicker). Action so far: I checked the river and Elon bet 2000 into a ~3050 pot (pot ≈5050 with his bet). Calling 2000 would commit me to a final pot of ~7050, so I need ~28.4% equity to break even (2000/7050 ≈ 28.4%).

Line reading and range: Elon opened BTN, c‑bet small on the flop, barreled the turn and now fires a large (~65% pot) river bet. That line is strongly weighted toward value (Kx, Jx, overpairs like TT/QQ/AA, occasional 9x/trips) with a smaller fraction of missed‑broadway/air bluffs. Given his consistent betting every street and the sizable river sizing, I expect >70% of his river‑betting range to beat a single pair of nines. Even generous estimates of his bluff frequency (≈25–30%) leave my calling equity below the ~28% threshold.

Options: Raising OOP with one pair is thin — it bloats the pot when I am likely behind and only gets called by better hands. Calling is only correct if I believe he is bluffing often enough; I don’t have a read that justifies that here. Folding preserves stack and avoids paying a negative‑EV river call.

Decision: Fold. I’ll conserve chips, wait for better spots or clearer bluff candidates, and avoid bloating the pot out of position with a marginal hand.

Button with A♠8♦ — a hand with a high-card ace and good positional advantage. I want initiative and fold equity against the blinds, so open-raise rather than limp. A 3x BB standard open to 300 accomplishes: isolates or folds the small blind, builds a pot when I'm likely ahead vs their calling ranges, and keeps the pot manageable with my medium kicker. Deep stacks let me play postflop for value and leverage position. If faced with a large 3-bet I will re-evaluate/fold; if called I'll use position to probe on favorable flops.

I have been dealt pocket kings (Kh Kc) in the small blind. This is the second-best starting hand in poker. Minni has raised to 300 from the button. My goal is to build a large pot and potentially get all-in pre-flop against a strong range. A standard 3-bet size here from the small blind against a 3x button open would be around 3.5x to 4x the initial raise, especially given the stack sizes. I will raise to 1100 to extract value and narrow the field.

Q8o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability out of position facing a 3-bet from the SB after a button open. The SB's 3-bet range is likely strong, including pairs, broadway, and suited aces, against which Q8o has poor equity and is difficult to play post-flop OOP. Pot odds are about 1.5:1 effective (1000 to call into 2500), but the implied odds aren't favorable with this hand. Folding preserves my stack for better spots in this long session.

I have A♠8♦ on the button facing a 3-bet to 1,100 after my open to 300. With ~88bb effective and position, A8s has good playability and useful blockers to broadway aces — calling keeps the pot controllable and lets me use position postflop. The immediate pot odds (call 800 into ~1,500) and villain’s aggressive tendencies make a call standard here; 4-betting would polarize me and often face stronger value hands (QQ+/AK) or a shove. Plan: call (1100 total) and evaluate on the flop — bet for value when I hit or have strong equity/draws, fold or proceed cautiously if I’m clearly behind to heavy aggression.

I have pocket kings (Kh Kc) on a very dry flop of Jc 6s 2h. I was the pre-flop 3-bettor and Minni called. This board is extremely favorable for my range as I have all the overpairs (AA, KK, QQ) and AJ/KJ combinations, while Minni's range contains many suited connectors and small-to-medium pairs that might have missed or are now marginal. I will bet for value and to protect my equity against any potential backdoor draws. A bet of about 1/3 to 1/2 the pot is standard here. I will bet 850.

Flash 3‑bet me pre and now fires a small c‑bet (850 into a 3,150 pot). Calling 850 gives me 4.7:1 pot odds (850/(3,150+850)=21.25% equity needed). The flop (2♥ J♣ 6♠) is pretty dry and my A♠8♦ has good blocker value to Ax combos and reasonable showdown value vs a wide c‑betting range. A raise would polarize me to strong value or bluffs I don’t block and is unnecessary here; folding is too weak given the price and my position. I’ll call to use position, realise equity, and evaluate the turn — if he checks I can take the initiative, if he barrels big I can fold unless I improve. Calling 850 (1950 total committed) is the best line.

I hold an overpair (KK) on a board of Jc 6s 2h 2s. The turn card (2s) is relatively safe, as it's unlikely Minni called a 3-bet pre-flop and a flop bet with a hand containing a deuce, unless it's exactly A2s. My goal is to continue extracting value from Jx combinations (like AJ, QJ, JT) and middle pocket pairs (77-TT). I will bet 1800 into the 4000 pot to keep the sizing consistent and set up a shove on the river, given Minni's remaining stack.

Call. The turn bet is 1,800 into a 5,800 pot so I need ~1800/(5800+1800)=~23.7% equity to justify a call. A♠8♦ has useful blockers to Ax combos and decent showdown value vs a wide, aggressive 3‑bet/c-bet/barrel range — and Flash’s turn stab is fairly small, which tells me his range likely includes a lot of thin value and bluffs. Folding here would be too nitty given the price; raising to the minimum (3600 total) would polarize me into a range I can’t credibly represent and would commit chips with a marginal hand. I’ll call 1,800 more (3750 total committed) to keep the pot controllable, use position on the river, and re-evaluate: fold to a large shove unless I improve, call smaller/medium river bets, or extract value if I pair up.

I have pocket kings (Kh Kc) on a board of 2h-Jc-6s-2s-4d. This is an extremely safe runout for my overpair. Minni has called a 3-bet pre-flop and two streets of value bets, which strongly suggests she holds a Jack (AJ, QJ, KJ, JT) or potentially a medium pocket pair (77-TT). Given the pot is 7600 and Minni has 5350 remaining, a bet of 5350 (her remaining stack) is the most profitable move. She is pot-committed with top pair and is unlikely to find a fold given the pot odds, whereas checking might allow her to check back with hands she would have called a bet with. I am losing only to rare slow-played sets (JJ, 66) or an improbable 2x.

I’m facing a 5,350 river shove and it would cost my remaining stack (all-in) to call. Pot before the bet is 12,950, so calling 5,350 is trying to win ~18,300 — I need ~29% equity to break even. I hold A♠8♦ and did not pair; against an opponent who 3‑bet pre, continued on the flop and turn, then sizes up big on the river, his range is heavily weighted toward value (JJ, QQ+, AK, Jx, possible 22/set combos) that crush my A‑high. Only pure bluffs beat me, and I don’t put his bluff frequency high enough to justify an all‑in call. I planned earlier to fold to a large river shove without improving, and preserving my 5,350 stack to pick better spots is the correct long‑term play. Fold.

I am on the button with 9-4 offsuit, which is a very weak starting hand. While button opening ranges are typically wide, this specific hand is near the bottom of any reasonable range. With Minni as the short stack in the big blind, she may be more inclined to defend or shove, and Elon is in the small blind. There is no need to play such a weak hand in this spot.

Hand 2h6d is extremely weak: offsuit low cards, poor playability post-flop. In SB vs BB, pot odds are 150:50 (3:1), but equity against any reasonable BB defending range is far too low (~20-30% at best). No steal value with this trash hand; Minni shows aggression in history but can call/raise light. Preserve stack for better spots. Standard fold.

Preflop on the button with 9d8s offsuit. This is a speculative one-gapper with decent playability for straights and potential two-pair hands, and I have excellent position. Facing a short-stacked SB limp from Minni (5350 stack, ~53BB) and a BB check from deep-stacked Flash. The pot is small at 150, and calling 100 gives great implied odds if I hit. However, raising builds the pot, can isolate the shorter Minni who may defend wide, and takes initiative. Folding is too tight given position and dynamics. Raising to 400 (4x the BB) is a standard isolating size against the limper while keeping it affordable.

I’m in the small blind with K♦3♦ (K3o) facing a button open to 400. Pot is 550 and it costs me 350 more to continue. K3o is a marginal, poorly playable hand out of position, so a 3‑bet or big isolation raise is not appropriate. However the price to see a flop is small (calling 350 into 550 gives ~39% break‑even equity), so I’ll defend the blind and realize equity. Plan: call and play the flop cautiously — fold to heavy aggression unless I pair my King or pick up strong draws; if I hit top pair or a good draw I can continue for value/initiative.

I am in the Big Blind with 5h 3c. The action is an open from the Button (Elon) to 400 and a call from the Small Blind (Minni). I have to put in 300 more to call into a pot that will be 1200. This is a very weak hand with poor playability, and although the price is somewhat tempting, playing out of position against two opponents with 5-high is generally a losing play in the long run. Given Minni's short stack, I want to preserve chips and wait for a better spot.

I defended the blind preflop with K3 to realize equity, but the flop (4♣ 2♣ J♦) completely misses me — I have only King‑high and no meaningful draw. I'm out of position vs the opener on a dry, uncoordinated board; leading or bluffing here is unnecessarily risky and easily exploited. I’ll check and control the pot: call small continuation bets to see a cheap turn only, fold to large pressure, and check‑down if he shows weakness. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot to fight for the pot.

Preflop I raised with 98o from the button for isolation and position, and Minni called from SB with a shorter stack of ~50BB. Flop Jd4c2c is a dry board with a weak club flush draw possible, low coordinated cards. My hand completely missed: no pair, no strong draws (gutshot unlikely, backdoor at best). However, as the preflop aggressor in position heads-up, a continuation bet has excellent fold equity against Minni's wide SB calling range (suited aces, broadway, connectors that missed). The board favors bluffs, blocks some Jx and straight draw hands like T9. Her check suggests weakness. Betting ~55% pot (500 into 900) balances value (if she has weak Jx/draws) and pressure, denying equity. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws and loses value when ahead. With deep effective stacks (her 5000), implied odds not an issue for bluff. Standard c-bet spot to maintain initiative.

I defended the small blind preflop to realize equity, but the flop (4♣ 2♣ J♦) missed my K♥3♦ completely — I have only king‑high and no meaningful draw. Elon’s bet of 500 into the 900 pot (pot now 1400) means calling 500 requires ~35.7% equity to break even. My hand only has ~12–13% chance to hit a king by showdown and has very little showdown value vs a BTN c‑betting range (Jx, Ax, broadways, bluffs). Out of position facing a sizable bet with negligible equity or fold equity, folding is the correct, stack‑preserving play.

I’m on the button in a 3-handed game with Q♦5♥ — a marginal hand but position is powerful. Opening here is standard and wins the blinds a lot; limp/calling 100 gives no fold equity and invites multiway action against two deep, aggressive stacks. I’ll make a standard button open to 300 (3×BB on this table) to apply pressure and keep the initiative. Plan: fold to a large 3‑bet, but if called I’ll play postflop in position (c‑bet light on dry/low boards, pot‑control or check vs heavy resistance on A‑high and Broadway textures). Folding is too tight and a min‑raise to 200 is weaker — 300 is the right balance for fold equity and postflop playability.

I am in the Small Blind with 9d 6d. Minni (the Button) has raised to 300. While 96 suited has some playability, I am out of position against a player who has shown aggression and a willingness to follow through on later streets. Furthermore, Minni's stack is relatively short (47 BB), which reduces the implied odds of my speculative hand. Elon in the Big Blind is also an aggressive player who could squeeze. Folding is the most prudent play to preserve my stack.

6h Th is a suited connector with good potential for strong draws and made hands post-flop. Facing a button raise from Minni, who has been aggressive and likely wide-ranging, my equity is solid heads-up (around 40% vs a typical button open). Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 more into 450 (laying 2.25:1 effectively, but with implied odds from effective stacks). OOP but SPR ~7 with Minni's 4700 stack allows playable spot. Folding too tight, 3-betting marginal as this hand prefers multi-street playability over polarization. Will call and see flop, looking to stack off if we hit flush/straight draws or better.

Flop of 3d Kh 2c completely misses my 6h Th suited connector. No pair, no strong draw—just backdoor straight/flush possibilities with minimal equity (~20-30% vs Minni's button range, which hits this K-high dry board frequently with Kx, pairs, Ax). Pot is 650, effective stacks Minni 4700 (SPR ~7), but leading into the aggressor OOP with air is -EV; induces raises and no fold equity. Checking allows pot control, realization of equity if she checks back (common vs missed draws), or cheap fold to c-bet. Folding pre was tight but defensible wide BB defense; now check-fold to preserve stack long-term.

I opened preflop and have position; flop (3♦ K♥ 2♣) is very dry and gives me lots of fold equity as the preflop aggressor. Q♦5♥ is just queen‑high with no real draw, so this is a pure bluff spot. Small continuation bet pressures many of the BB’s calling range (missed broadways, weak Ax, small pairs) while keeping the pot controllable if called. I’ll fire ~1/3–40% pot (250 into 650). Plan: take the pot if he folds; if called, re‑evaluate on the turn and fold to significant resistance (check‑raise or large bet) since my equity vs Kx/overpairs is poor.

Flop completely misses my 6h Th suited connector: no pair, no strong draw, only backdoor heart flush and vague straight possibilities. Equity is low (~25% max vs Minni's wide button opening range, which connects well with Kx, Ax, pairs, broadway on this K-high coordinated but dry board with 3d 2c). Pot 900, facing 250 bet (1/3 pot sizing), calling lays poor odds without meaningful implied odds or reverse implied risk given her aggression history. Raising as a bluff OOP is high-variance with no fold equity vs her capping range; she'd continue wide with floats or value. Check-fold preserves stack for better spots in long 150-hand game, avoiding bloated pot and turn decisions with air. Pre-flop call was fine for multiway potential/speculative value, but realization forces discipline.

I am on the button with 7-3 offsuit, which is one of the weakest hands in poker. There is no reason to enter the pot with such a poor hand, regardless of position or stack size. Folding is the standard and correct play here.

Preflop in SB with 74o, a trash hand with poor playability, low equity, and bad implied odds. Flash folded, so heads-up vs BB Minni, but completing 50 into 150 pot (3:1) doesn't justify it given the hand's weakness. Standard fold to preserve stack for better opportunities.

3-handed on the button with K8o. Excellent position. Opening range from button in 3-handed play is very wide, and K8o is well within it due to king blocker, playability, and potential for top pair top kicker. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) is standard to take initiative, deny equity to blinds, and build the pot while maintaining fold equity. Limping is suboptimal as it invites multiway pots without initiative; folding is too tight given position and hand strength. Post-flop position allows flexible play. Opponent stacks allow for standard sizing without overcommitting.

I’m in the small blind with 7♥4♥ facing a button open to 300 (I’ve already posted 50). Calling costs 250 into a 450 pot, so I’d need roughly 36% equity to justify a call. 7‑4 suited has very poor equity against a BTN opening range and is dominated by most hands that will continue (broadway cards, pairs, higher hearts). With the big blind still to act I’m likely to face a multiway pot or a squeeze, and I’ll be out of position post‑flop with a very weak hand. A raise here would be a thin, high‑variance bluff with little blocker or fold equity. I’ll preserve my 5,350 stack for better spots and fold.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Queen offsuit, a very strong hand in a three-handed game. The button (Elon) has opened to 300. To take control of the hand and extract value from weaker kings, queens, or middle-range hands that might call, I will put in a three-bet. Raising to 1000 is a standard sizing to discourage a wide range of bluffs while building a pot for a hand that has significant equity.

Facing a 3-bet from Flash in the BB after my button open-raise to 300 with K8o. In 3-handed play, BB 3-bet ranges are strong for value (QQ+, AKo, AQs+, maybe some suited broadways), and K8o has poor equity against that: dominated by better kings, crushed by pairs. Offsuit reduces playability postflop despite position. Pot odds to call 700 into 1350 (~1.9:1) are decent, but implied odds are risky given hand's vulnerability to overcards and domination. 4-betting as bluff has some merit with K blocker, but 8d is a poor blocker, and Flash's history shows strength when 3-betting (e.g., quads in #83, value heavy pots). Minni folded, heads-up vs deep-stacked aggressive opponent; folding preserves stack for better spots. Wide opening range doesn't mandate defense here; disciplined fold maximizes EV long-term.

I’m on the button with Q3o — a marginal but playable hand in position. With only the blinds to face, I want to seize the initiative and apply pressure to win the pot preflop or play a slimmer postflop range in position. A standard 3xBB open to 300 gives good fold equity versus the SB/BB and keeps the pot manageable with 53.5BB effective stack behind. If I face a cold 3-bet from either opponent I’ll let this one go; if called I’ll C-bet on dry/Q-high flops and proceed cautiously on coordinated or heavy-draw boards.

I am in the Small Blind with 84o, which is a very weak hand. Minni opened from the button for 300. Even though the price to call is 250, 84o does not play well out of position and has very little post-flop equity. Standard professional strategy dictates a fold here to preserve chips for better opportunities.

AhJh is a premium suited Broadway hand with excellent playability, positionally defended in the BB against a button min-raise from Minni, who has shown aggression but a wide range in history. Pot odds are great (200 into 450), but 3-betting applies pressure, builds the pot for value against worse Aces, broadways, and suited hands in her range, while denying equity to floats. Sizing to 900 (3x) is standard and balanced given stacks (mine 15275, hers 5050). Calling is fine but raising maximizes value long-term in this multi-hand game.

I opened to 300 on the button but Elon responded with a 3-bet to 900. Qh3d is a very weak, often-dominated hand vs a typical 3-bet range — it has poor playability and few useful blockers to strong hands. With ~50BB effective, a 4-bet bluff isn’t attractive and calling would put me in tough postflop spots against an opponent who has shown frequent preflop aggression. Preserve my stack and wait for a better spot — fold here.

I am on the button with Ten-Nine offsuit, which is a standard opening hand in a three-handed game. With both opponents in the blinds, raising allows me to take control of the pot and potentially win the blinds immediately. My stack is healthy, and while Minni is relatively short-stacked, T9o has enough playability to warrant an open to 300.

54h suited connectors have some potential for straight and flush draws, and the pot odds are decent (calling 250 into 450, ~2.8:1 direct, better if BB folds). However, as SB, I'm OOP postflop against Flash's button range, which is wide but strong. History shows Flash raises frequently from late position and can barrel effectively. Minni in BB is short-stacked (49BB) and defends wide sometimes, risking multiway pots where equity drops. Deep stacks allow for better spots; folding avoids tough OOP decisions and realizes EV by waiting for premium hands or better position. Standard fold for 54s OOP vs BTN open.

I’m in the big blind with K♠6♣ facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and it costs me 200 to call (300 total). K6o is marginal and often dominated, but the price is correct: calling 200 into 450 (~3.25:1) vs a wide/aggressive button range gives me defendable equity. I have ~49.5bb effective, so calling is cheap and keeps the pot manageable OOP. 3-betting with K6o is suboptimal (poor blockers and bad postflop playability), and folding is too tight given the odds. Plan: call to see a flop, fold to a large preflop 4‑bet, and proceed cautiously on the flop — look to continue with top-pair or good textures, otherwise check/fold or float selectively against small C-bets.

Flop: Qc 5s As. I hold K♠6♣ — no pair and essentially no realistic draw (I have one spade; with two spades on board I’d need both turn and river to make a flush). Flash was the preflop raiser and has a wide, aggressive button range that contains many Ax, broadways and hands that will C‑bet this texture. Betting here for thin value is poor and a small blocker bet has little fold equity and only invites raises that I must fold to. Pot is 650 and I’m out of position with ~47.5bb — best line is to keep the pot small and proceed cautiously. Plan: check and fold to significant aggression; only continue if I pick up a pair (K on turn) or see a very small float attempt I can exploit later. Action: check.

On a board of Qc-5s-As, I have a significant range advantage as the pre-flop aggressor, as my range contains more strong Aces and Queens. Although I have no direct equity with Ten-Nine offsuit, this is a prime board for a continuation bet. I will bet around half the pot to put pressure on Minni's marginal holdings like middle pairs or weak draws, representing the Ace myself.

I checked the flop as planned and now face Flash’s 325 into a 975 pot. Calling 325 requires ~25% equity to break even, but K♠6♣ has almost no showdown value here (dominated by Ax and Qx in a button’s C-bet range) and only a remote runner-runner spade possibility. I’m out of position against an aggressive, deeper-stacked opponent who can comfortably barrel turns/rivers, so calling to float is low EV and playing out of position with nothing is unattractive. Fold and preserve my stack for better spots.

3-handed on the button facing an unraised pot (standard blinds posted, to call the BB). 7d8c is offsuit connectors with good playability for straights and two-pair hands, excellent in position against blinds. Opponents have shown aggression but deep stacks allow post-flop maneuvering. Raising steals blinds frequently or builds pot when called. Standard 3x raise to 300 balances range and applies pressure.

Facing a 300 open from the button with the small blind I would need to call 250 (300 total) into a 450 pot. That means my call requires ~35.7% equity to break even (250 / (450+250)). Q♠6♦ is a weak offsuit queen that is heavily dominated by typical button opening ranges (lots of Qx/Kx/Ax) and plays very poorly out of position against both opener and the big blind. A reraise here would be pure bluff and unnecessarily bloats the pot out of position; calling turns this into a tough 3‑way OOP spot where I’ll be behind a large portion of ranges. With 47bb effective and many better spots to pick up chips, the +EV play is to conserve chips and fold.

I am in the Big Blind holding Qc 9c. Elon, on the button, has raised to 300. This is a standard late-position open. Qc 9c is a strong suited connector that plays very well post-flop, especially in a three-way or heads-up pot. The pot odds are favorable: I need to call 200 to win a pot of 450. Calling is standard here to see a flop, though a 3-bet is occasionally an option; however, given the stack sizes and the tendency for Elon to be aggressive, calling allows me to realize my equity and play a medium-sized pot in position relative to the board's development.

I have flopped a monster draw with an open-ended straight draw and a flush draw, giving me 15 clean outs to a very strong hand. Being out of position against Elon, who has shown significant aggression throughout the game, the best play is to check. This allows him to follow through with a continuation bet, at which point I can decide whether to call to see the turn or check-raise to maximize value and fold equity given my massive amount of equity.

Preflop raiser on the button vs BB defender who checked flop. Board Jc5cTs is coordinated with flush and straight draws possible (e.g., 89o/e, Q9, AcXc), but hits BB calling range moderately (Jx, Tx, suited connectors). My 78o misses completely but has minor backdoor straight/flush potential. As aggressor in position with range advantage (perceived AQo+, KQo+, suited broadways, pairs), standard to c-bet for value from Jx/Tx/overpairs, protection, and fold equity against floats/draws/marginal hands. Flash's history shows calling PF but folding to cbets often (e.g., Hands 2,9,15,17). Pot 650, bet ~60% (400) balances thin value/protection/fold equity without overbetting deep stacks. Denies equity to draws cheaply.

I have flopped a massive combo draw with an open-ended straight draw (8, K) and a flush draw, giving me 15 outs. Against almost any hand in Elon's button-opening range, I have approximately 54% equity. By check-raising here, I maximize my fold equity against his air and marginal hands while building a large pot for the times I hit one of my many outs. Given Elon's frequent aggression, a semi-bluff raise is the most profitable play to capitalize on my high equity and take control of the hand.

Facing a check-raise on a coordinated flop Jc5cTs from the BB defender who called my button open preflop. My 7d8c is complete air with no pair, no straight draw (78 doesn't connect to T-J-5 for gutshot or OESD), only insignificant backdoor club flush draw via 8c. Board hits BB calling range hard (Jx, Tx, sets like JJ/TT/55, combo draws AcXc/9cXc, 89 suited for open-ender). Check-raise represents strength: value from top pair+, protection, or semibluff with strong equity. My button raising range is polarized but perceived stronger; however, continuing into check-raise with zero equity is -EV. Pot odds 2250:800 (~3:1) insufficient vs villain's range where I have <25% equity. Deep stacks but no fold equity on me now, and multiway potential gone (SB folded). Standard fold preserves stack for better spots. Opponent history shows Flash defends BB wide but check-raises aggressively when strong (e.g., Hands 2,9,15 where Flash folded to cbets but here fights back).

Button with 4♣ T♠ and 47bb effective — this is a marginal hand but benefits a lot from position. I want to seize initiative and try to steal the blinds rather than limp/pass and let the blinds dictate action. A standard 3x-ish open to 300 (total) is table-norm here, gives decent fold equity versus the blinds, keeps the pot manageable vs these deeper stacks, and lets me play postflop in position. If I face a large 3-bet I’ll fold and preserve the stack; if called I’ll c-bet with plan to continue when I pick up equity or fold to heavy resistance.

I am in the Small Blind with QJ offsuit, a strong broadway hand in a three-handed game. Minni, who is on the button with a relatively short stack of 4400 (44 BB), has raised to 300. In this position, I want to either fold or 3-bet to avoid being out of position in a multi-way pot and to take the initiative against a likely wide button opening range. QJo is too strong to fold here. By raising to 1000, I put pressure on Minni's range and likely force the Big Blind to fold, allowing me to isolate the shorter stack or win the pot immediately.

Pocket kings are an extremely strong hand preflop, second only to AA. Minni's button open-raise to 300 is likely wide, including many speculative hands. Flash's SB 3-bet to 1000 could be polarized (strong value or bluffs), but SB 3-bets are often wider than other positions. Folding KK here is never correct. Calling OOP puts me at a positional disadvantage multiway and allows Minni to 4-bet. 4-betting for value thins the field, builds a pot with our premium hand, and pressures both opponents. Sizing to 2500 (2.5x the 3-bet) is standard, denies equity to worse hands, and leaves us well-stacked post-flop. Minni's short stack (4400) adds fold equity if they have marginal hands.

Opened to 300 to seize position, but now facing a 3-bet to 1000 (SB) and a 4-bet to 2500 (BB). Pot is 3800 and I must call 2200 (2500 total) which gives ~36.7% pot odds, but this is a three-way pot against very strong, polarized ranges. T♠4♣ is heavily dominated multiway and has very poor realization equity here. I could consider a shove/5-bet, but a raise to the 4000 min would commit almost my entire 4400 stack with a weak hand vs two likely value ranges — high variance and bad expectation. I planned preflop to fold to large resistance; preserve the 44bb for better spots and fold.

I am facing a cold 4-bet from Elon in the Big Blind after I 3-bet Minni's button raise. While QJo is a strong hand in 3-handed play, it is significantly behind a standard 4-bet range which usually consists of premium pairs and strong Ace-X hands (AK, AQ). Calling 1,500 more out of position with a hand that is likely dominated or at best a coin flip against a lower pair is a losing play. Given Elon's stack size and the strength shown by 4-betting into two opponents, folding is the most disciplined and correct move to preserve my stack.

I am on the button with 7-6 offsuit. While this can occasionally be an opening hand in a three-handed game, the table dynamics make it less appealing here. Elon (SB) has been playing very aggressively with frequent 3-bets, and Minni (BB) is short-stacked with 43 big blinds, making her more likely to shove or defend tenaciously. Given 7-6 offsuit's poor equity against their continuing ranges and the high likelihood of facing pressure, I will choose the low-variance play and fold to wait for a better spot.

Unopened pot (button folded) heads-up SB vs BB. T4s suited connector has solid equity (~40% vs loose BB calling range), good implied odds if connects, and fold equity for steal. Standard play in SB is to raise speculative suited hands for initiative OOP. Minni aggressive but defends wide; sizing to 3x BB (300 total) balances value/thin value/steal. Effective stacks deep enough (Minni 43BB), but positionally we take control preflop. Calling limps OOP passively; folding too tight given pot odds (3:1). Raising maximizes EV long-term.

Elon opened to 300 from the small blind; pot is 400 and I need 200 more to call. I have 6♣8♦ — a marginal, connected offsuit hand with some post‑flop playability but no real blockers. With my stack (~43 BB) I don’t want to inflate the pot out of position by 3‑betting as a bluff (min‑raise to 500 would commit more and give Elon good opportunities to shove or apply pressure). Calling is cheap here: I’m investing 200 to contest a 600 pot (reasonable pot odds) against a likely wide SB opening range, and the hand can flop straights/2‑pair/draws that play well heads‑up. Plan: call to see a flop, continue with pairs or strong draws, fold to heavy turn/river aggression if I miss.

Preflop SB raise with suited connector T4c took initiative OOP and got called by Minni in BB, who defends wide based on history. Flop Ks9c2c rainbow except two clubs hits my exact club flush draw (9 outs to nuts, ~35% equity). No pair/set/straight draw but board favors my perceived strong range (Kx, suited Ax/broadways/connectors). OOP first to act, checking risks free card to her equity (possible flush draws, str8 draws, pairs). Semi-bluff pot-sized bet maximizes fold equity against her weak Kx, overpairs, marginal calls; if called/shoved, realize equity with good implied odds (her 41BB stack). Pot odds for her poor if marginal (600 into 1200 needs 33%+). High EV play balancing value/bluff.

I fold. I called preflop to see a flop but this K♠-9♣-2♣ board completely misses my 6♣8♦: I have no pair, no real draw (only a single backdoor club and runner-runner possibilities). Elon’s 600 into a 1200 pot requires ~33% equity to justify a call — my hand’s equity versus a typical SB c-bet range (Kx, club draws, broadways, small pairs) is well below that. Raising would be a thin, easily punished bluff OOP and calling to float OOP with no plan on most turns is negative EV. With ~41 BB I preserve chips for better spots rather than investing in a marginal, drawless holding, so I fold.

K6s is a strong suited king hand from the button with excellent playability and position. Facing a small bet of 100 into 150 pot (likely a limp or complete), raising isolates the short-stacked SB (Minni with 40BB) who may fold weak hands or shove, while the deep BB (Flash) can be played against postflop. Standard raise size to 3x the effective BB/call for value and fold equity, building the pot in position. Folding or calling is too passive with this hand's potential.

Fold. Facing a button open to 300 I have Q♦4♥ in the small blind — a weak, often-dominated offsuit queen. Calling costs 250 more into a 450 pot, which requires roughly 36% equity (250/700 ≈ 35.7%) to break even; with the big blind yet to act and out-of-position play likely, my effective equity is lower. A min 3-bet is too thin to fold out quality hands and an all-in is overcommitting with a marginal holding. Preserve the ~40BB stack for a better spot — fold and concede the small blind.

I am in the Big Blind with 8c 3s, which is an extremely weak hand. Elon raised from the button, and Minni folded from the Small Blind. Although I am getting 2.25-to-1 odds to call, this hand is poorly connected and has no potential to make a strong hand. Given the strength of my current stack and the risk of playing a junk hand out of position, the best strategic move is to fold.

On the button with T♠2♥ — a weak, unsuited holding, but I have positional advantage and 40 BB effective. I want to leverage position and apply pressure to the blinds rather than limp and play a multiway pot out of position. A standard 3x open to 300 (rather than the bare min to 200) gives reasonable fold equity and avoids giving cheap odds to the blinds. If I face a large 3-bet I’ll fold this hand; if called I’ll proceed cautiously in position (c-bet favorable flops, pot-control or fold to strong resistance on dry/unfavorable boards). Raise to 300 to steal or take the initiative in position.

I am in the Small Blind with 92o, which is one of the weakest hands in poker. Minni has raised from the button to 300. Given the strength of my hand and being out of position, there is no reason to continue. I will fold.

Preflop in the BB facing a button raise to 300 after SB folds. Pot is 450, calling 200 more for great odds (pot will be 650). QTo offsuit is a strong defensive hand with good playability: straight potential, broadway cards, blocks QJs/QKs. Minni's button range is wide (broadway, suited connectors, Ax), so we have decent equity (~40%+) heads-up. Postflop OOP but deep effective stacks (Minni 37.5BB), we can realize equity well. Folding too tight; 3-betting risks getting shoved on by her wide range where QTo is not a favorite. Calling keeps pot small, sees flop cheap, and allows postflop flexibility. History shows Minni raises frequently from late position, often marginal hands.

Flop Jc 2s 9s gives me complete air: Queen-high with no pair, no straight draw (T-Q doesn't connect meaningfully with J-9-2), no flush draw. Spade-heavy board favors Minni's wide button raising range (Jx, 9x, spade flush draws like AsXs, KsQs suited connectors). OOP first to act, pot 650, her effective stack 3750 (~5.75 pot-sized). Betting air risks folding out better and gets called by worse rarely. Standard check-fold: she c-bets frequently (~60-70% on such dry-ish but drawy boards per history of aggressive play). Folding to bet preserves stack; occasional check-behind allows cheap river if she slows down. No bluff-raising value with this blocker-poor hand vs her range. Prioritizing EV by avoiding unnecessary spew deep.

I have T♠2♥ on J♣ 2♠ 9♠ — bottom pair (pair of deuces) with a weak kicker. Elon (BB) called preflop so his range is wide (overcards, medium pairs, some draws and spade combos). The flop is semi‑wet with two spades (he may have spade draws) but I’m in position and have the initiative from my open. A small continuation bet (~1/3 pot) is the best play: it gets value/folds from worse/unpaired hands, charges spade and straight draws, and keeps the pot manageable given my 37.5BB effective stack. If he raises big I’ll fold (hand too vulnerable); if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and proceed cautiously (check-call blanks, fold to heavy turn aggression unless I improve). Betting 225 chips strikes the balance between fold equity and pot control.

On this Jc2s9s flop, QcTd is complete air: queen-high with no pair, no straight draw (Q-T misses connections to J-9-2 for gutshot or OESD), no flush draw, and only backdoor equity at best. Facing Minni's 225 bet into 875 pot (~1/4 pot) from the button after her wide preflop raising range, which hits this board frequently (Jx, 9x, suited spades/connectors like KQs, Axs). History shows Minni c-bets aggressively postflop (~60-70% frequency on coordinated boards). OOP check-fold is standard: calling builds pot OOP with ~25% equity at best vs her polarized range, facing further barrels or turn cards that improve her; no implied odds deep but realizes nothing. Raising as bluff has poor blockers (no J/9 block), low fold equity vs her sticky calling station tendencies seen in prior hands (e.g., calls large with draws/secondaries). Preserves stack for better spots in deep 150-hand game; spewing here negative EV.

I am on the button with pocket deuces in a 3-handed game. Any pocket pair is a standard opening hand from the button. While Minni is relatively short-stacked at 43 big blinds, which increases the likelihood of a shove, I should still open to 300 to pick up the blinds or see a flop. If I face a 3-bet from Elon or a shove from Minni, I will re-evaluate, but 22 is strong enough to start the action here.

AQo is a strong premium hand warranting a 3-bet from the small blind against a button open-raise. Flash's button raise is likely wide and steal-oriented based on history of aggression from late position. 3-betting applies fold equity, isolates if called, and gets value from worse aces, broadway, suited connectors that Flash might flat. Postflop OOP but deep stacks allow flexibility. Minni's shorter stack (43BB) may call or shove weaker, but AQ dominates much of her range. Sizing to 900 (3x opener, ~2.25x pot) is standard for value and pressure.

Facing a BTN open to 300 and a SB 3-bet to 900, I’m in the BB with 2♥3♠. Pot is 1,300 and I must call 800 more (900 total) or fold. Calling would require roughly ~38% equity to break even and I’d be out of position vs two active opponents in a multiwaypot — 2-3 offsuit has very poor equity against a 3-bet range and virtually no blockers for a credible 4-bet bluff. With a 4,300 stack I should conserve chips for better spots and avoid playing a marginal hand OOP multiway against aggression. Fold and preserve the stack.

I am holding pocket deuces on the button. I opened the action for 300, and Elon has 3-bet from the small blind to 900. While pocket pairs have value, set-mining against a 3-bet in a 3-handed game can be tricky, especially with 22 which is frequently dominated and rarely the best hand unimproved. However, we are playing deep-stacked (both over 150 BBs), and the price to call is 600 more to win a total pot of 1300. This provides decent implied odds if I hit a set on the flop. Given the stack depths and the fact that 3-handed play involves wider ranges, calling to see a flop is a viable play here.

Preflop 3-bet with AQo was standard for value and isolation against Flash's wide button open. Flop 7c-3c-5d is a low, draw-heavy board (flush draw, wheel str8 draws like 64, 42, OESD 46/24/34) that misses much of Flash's calling range (Ax without low cards, broadway, medium pairs like 88-JJ, suited broadway). My AcQs is ace-high with strong blockers to AA/AK/AQ/A5 (Ac blocks nut flush/ str8 draws), giving fold equity. As OOP 3-bettor, c-bet ~2/3 pot denies equity to draws/floats, targets his medium strength, maintains range advantage (my range hits sets, two-pair, flush draws, A5s/A3s/A7s). Flash folds to aggression postflop often (e.g., Hand 2,9,17,27,53, etc.). Deep stacks (90+BB), good SPR post-bet. Check risks giving free cards or facing bets without clear plan; betting polarizes effectively.

Elon 3-bet from the small blind pre-flop and is now following up with a strong continuation bet of approximately 68% of the pot on a low, somewhat connected board. My pocket deuces are an underpair to the board and are extremely vulnerable to almost any hand in Elon's 3-betting range, including overpairs like 88-AA or even overcards that have significant equity against me. I am not getting the implied odds to chase a set, and since 22 is at the very bottom of my range in this spot, folding is the most disciplined and mathematically sound decision.

3-handed on the button with KTo offsuit after presumed SB fold (no actions logged, pot=150 standard blinds). Excellent position deep stacked vs BB. KTo has good equity, playability postflop, and strong blocker to top pairs. Standard to raise for value/thin value/isolation/steal blinds. Size to 3x BB (300 total), minraise is 200 but 300 is standard to build pot or take it down. Avoid limping or calling to maintain initiative.

Elon opens to 300 from the button. I’m in the small blind with T♦J♠ (offsuit) and 42.5BB effective. This is a defendable hand but not a strong 3-bet candidate out of position — 3-betting here would bloat the pot OOP and be too thin. Folding is too tight vs a standard BTN open. I'll call the additional 250 (bringing my total contribution to 300) to see a flop and play postflop for value or pot-control depending on how the big blind acts.

I am in the Big Blind holding Ten of Hearts and Nine of Clubs. Elon raised from the Button to 300, and Minni called from the Small Blind. The pot is currently 700, and it costs me 200 more to call. This gives me 3.5:1 pot odds (22% equity required). While T9o is not a premium hand, it has decent connectivity and I am getting a very good price to see a flop in a three-way pot. Minni's short stack (40bb) makes the hand slightly more complex, but calling is the most profitable play given the odds.

Multiway flop (btn open + BB call), I’m out of position with T♦J♠ on A♠-4♣-K♣. This is a dry, high-card texture and I have no club and no pair — just two medium overcards to the 4 and only a backdoor turned/rivered straight (would need Q+9). Betting OOP as a bluff into two opponents is poor: I’m behind most of the BTN/BB ranges (Ax, Kx, broadways) and have very little fold equity or equity to improve. I’ll check and keep the pot controllable. If faced with a small continuation bet I’ll consider a call to see the turn, but I’ll fold to a large c-bet or raise since my equity is low. If checked through, I’ll reassess on the turn and only apply pressure if I pick up meaningful equity (Q, T, 9 or a safe card), otherwise continue to pot-control.

I have T9o on an AK4 board. This board heavily favors the pre-flop raiser's range (Elon) and I have no pair, no draw, and very little potential to improve. My goal is to check and see what the original raiser does. If there is any significant betting action, I will fold as my hand has virtually zero equity in this three-way pot.

Top pair with King on an Ace-high board with two clubs. I have the second nut King (Kh) and a Ten kicker, blocking some AK/AQ combos. Preflop raiser with position, both opponents check to me, indicating weakness or traps but likely weakness given multiway pot dynamics from history. Strong value betting spot to protect against club flush draws (9 outs), gutshots (unlikely), and extract from weaker Ax, Kx, floats. Minni is shorter (~40BB), Flash deep. Bet 2/3 pot to build pot, deny equity, get value from worse. Pot 900, bet 600 leaves good SPR postflop.

Elon (BTN) opened to 300 and I defended from the SB with T♦J♠ to see a flop. Flop A♠‑4♣‑K♣ was checked to Elon, who makes a sizable c‑bet of 600 into a 900 pot (multiway). I have no pair (only two overcards to the 4 but both are under the A and K), no realistic draw, and very little showdown equity versus a BTN c‑bet range that includes lots of Ax, Kx and strong broadways. Calling 600 to win 1500 gives ~28.6% break‑even equity, which I don’t have here, and raising OOP into two players is a poor strategy (minimal fold equity, bad blockers, and it would commit a large chunk of my ~40BB stack). Folding preserves my stack for better, position‑friendly spots.

I have Ten-high on a board featuring an Ace and a King. The pre-flop aggressor, Elon, has bet 600 into 900, which represents 2/3 of the pot. This board hits the button's opening range very hard. I have no pair, no draws, and no realistic way to win this hand. Continuing would be a significant mistake.

Button with J♠5♥ and a 4,000 stack (40bb). Pot is 150 and it’s last to act preflop — this is a spot to open-steal and play in position. J5o is marginal but playable on the button; I’ll raise to 300 total (3bb) to generate fold equity and keep the pot controllable. If either blind 3-bets I’ll fold (hand is too weak to defend big in a bloated pot), but if I get one or two callers I’ll play post-flop in position: c-bet small on dry, high-card boards, check and control on coordinated boards, and continue aggressively when I pair my J or pick up strong draws. Raise size balances steal attempt vs keeping stacks manageable against two deep/aggressive opponents.

I am in the Small Blind with Ad 8d, a strong suited ace in a three-handed game. The button (Minni) has opened for 300. Minni is relatively short-stacked with only 3,700 chips. 3-betting here is standard to isolate the opener, take control of the pot, and avoid playing a multi-way pot out of position against the Big Blind. A raise to 1,000 puts significant pressure on Minni's remaining stack while remaining priced for value against a wide button opening range.

Td2s is a trash hand with zero equity against a 3bet range from Flash after Minni's open-raise. No suited connectors, no high cards, poor postflop playability even OOP. Pot odds to call 900 into 2300 (~39%) don't justify it against strong ranges. Folding preserves stack for better spots with 30+ hands left.

Facing Flash's 3-bet to 1000 from the small blind after my 300 button open with J♠5♥. I have 3,700 behind, pot is ~1,400 and I must call 700 (getting ~2:1, so I need >33% equity). A typical SB 3‑bet range vs a button open is value-heavy (TT+, AQs+, AJs, KQs, strong Axs) with some bluffs; J5o is often dominated and likely has only ~20–25% equity vs that range. 4‑betting to 1,700 would commit too large a portion of my stack as a bluff and is unjustified without strong blockers. Calling to play postflop in position is marginal — with this hand I’ll be difficult to play against turn/river aggression and will face frequent domination. Goal over 150 hands is to preserve stack and pick better spots, so I fold here.

On the button with J8o in a 3-handed game, this hand is at the very bottom of a standard opening range. Considering Minni's relatively short stack (36BB) and Elon's frequent 3-betting history in recent hands, opening with a marginal hand like J8o invites a high-variance confrontation that I'd rather avoid. I will fold and look for a better spot.

Preflop in SB with J4o offsuit, a speculative trash hand with poor playability OOP. Flash folded, so facing BB Minni who plays aggressively and has a short stack of ~36BB. Pot odds to call 50 into 150 are decent (~3:1), but equity vs BB calling range is low (~30-35%), and postflop OOP with this hand is disastrous for multiway or vs raises. No reason to raise or limp; folding preserves stack for better spots in late game stages with deep stacks.

6-4 offsuit is a trash hand with poor playability, low equity, and no straight or flush potential. Preflop on the button facing a call of 100 into a 150 pot offers insufficient odds for such a weak holding. Deep stacks don't justify speculative play here. Opponents' stacks noted, but hand too weak to enter pot voluntarily. Standard fold to preserve stack.

Elon folded so I'm heads-up with Flash in the big blind. I have T♠ J♥ — a strong, playable hand heads-up that performs well in position postflop (I'm the dealer/small blind so I act last after the flop). Pot is 150 and calling 50 would be passive; raising to the minimum (200 total) takes the initiative, builds the pot with a hand that plays well in position, and can fold out worse hands. I'm aware Flash can 3-bet aggressively, and I'll re-evaluate if he does (likely fold to a large 3-bet given our stack depth), but opening to 200 is the standard, +EV play here.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Seven of spades, a strong hand for heads-up play against the Small Blind. Minni (Small Blind) has a relatively short stack of approximately 35.5 big blinds and has opened with a min-raise. Given my positional advantage for the rest of the hand and the strength of my suited Ace, I will put in a 3-bet to 600. This puts pressure on her wide opening range and allows me to take control of the pot. If she shoves, I will have to evaluate based on pot odds, but for now, a raise is the standard aggressive play.

I opened to 200 from the small blind with T♠ J♥ and Flash 3‑bet to 600. Facing 400 more into a pot of 800, the immediate pot odds (about 3:1) make a call attractive — TJo heads‑up in position has solid equity versus a 3‑bet range that contains broadways, suited Aces and pairs, and Flash has shown an aggressive 3‑betty style. 4‑betting to the minimum (1000) would unnecessarily polarize my range with a marginal hand, give poor fold equity vs an aggressive, deep‑stacked opponent, and lead to tougher decisions or facing a shove. Folding is too tight given the hand and the pot odds. I’ll call (600 total) to preserve my positional advantage, keep the pot manageable, and play postflop — fold to a shove or very large 4‑bet, and proceed with c‑bet / check‑back / raise lines depending on the flop texture.

Flop A♣ 9♠ 7♣ with T♠ J♥ in position — I missed. Versus Flash's preflop 3‑bet his range is heavy in Ax, broadways and pairs that connect well with this board, so a continuation bet has poor fold equity and would just bloat the pot with a marginal hand. I have only a gutshot to an 8 (4 clean outs) and limited backdoor equity, and my stack is ~3,150 (≈31 BB) so pot control is important. Checking keeps the initiative in position, lets me realize equity cheaply, and gives me chances to call small/medium bets or induce bluffs. Plan: check now; call small-to-medium c-bets to see the turn but fold to large, pot‑committing aggression (pot-sized bets or shoves) given my limited equity. If the turn meaningfully improves my hand (an 8 or a pair) I’ll then re-evaluate and can proceed more aggressively.

On the flop of Ac-7c-9s, I have flopped top and bottom pair (Aces and Sevens). This is a very strong hand. Minni has checked to me. There are potential flush draws (clubs) and straight draws (86, T8, JT) available. Given the pot size is 1200 and Minni only has 3150 remaining in her stack, I want to bet for value and to protect against draws. A bet of around half the pot is standard here to keep her wider range in, but since she is short-stacked, I am looking to eventually get the money in. I will bet 550.

I missed this flop with T♠J♥ on A♣9♠7♣. My only clean outs are the four 8s (≈16.5% to make the straight by river) and a few remote backdoor possibilities — well under the ~24% equity the pot is giving me (550 to call into a 1,750 pot). Flash 3‑bet preflop and is c‑betting this texture with a range heavy in Ax, broadways and pairs that dominate my hand. A raise would over‑polarize my range and risk a big 4‑bet or shove; a call is a thin, negative‑EV chase. Preserve my ~31BB stack and fold.

Button vs two deep, 31.5BB effective. Q♠3♣ is marginal but on the button I want to leverage position and steal the blinds rather than limp and let them take initiative. A standard 3x open (to 300) gives fold equity, keeps a compact pot if called, and is consistent with table sizing. If either blind 3-bets large, I'll fold—Q3o is not a hand I want to gamble a big portion of my stack with out of position. If called, I'll use position to c-bet favorable flops and keep pot control on bad boards. Raising to 300 maximizes short-term fold equity while preserving the ability to fold to heavy action and play postflop in position if necessary.

I am in the Small Blind with Qd Tc. The button (Minni) has raised to 300. Minni is extremely short-stacked with only 2850 chips remaining total. This hand is a standard defend in the small blind against a button open, but given Minni's short stack, I have to consider the high likelihood of an all-in confrontation. Qd Tc is a strong enough hand to continue here, and calling is generally better than raising to keep the pot controllable or induce bluffs, though folding is too tight. However, since the big blind (Elon) is a large stack and still to act, calling is somewhat risky. Given the pot odds (250 to call into a pot of 450), I will call and see a flop.

Ah6h is a strong suited ace from the big blind facing a button raise and small blind call. Button opens are wide, SB flat is often speculative. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 more into 700 (3.5:1, ~22% equity needed). Hand has good playability - nut flush draw potential, strong ace blocker, straight potential. OOP but deep stacks and implied odds justify calling to realize equity multiway or heads-up postflop. 3-betting is possible as a squeeze but risks multiway pot OOP with marginal hand; folding is too tight given position and odds. Standard call to see flop cheap.

On a monotone board of diamonds, I have top pair (Tens) and the Queen of diamonds, which gives me a draw to the second-nut flush. Being out of position against two opponents, one of whom is extremely short-stacked (Minni), checking is the most prudent move. This allows me to see how they react to the board. If Minni bets her remaining chips, I have plenty of equity to continue. If Elon (the big stack) bets, I can evaluate based on the sizing and Minni's action. Checking preserves my options and keeps the pot manageable while I hold a strong but vulnerable hand.

Flop of 7d5dTd is extremely wet with a flush draw and multiple straight draws (open-enders like 86, 68, 89, J9). My Ah6h has no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw - just ace-high with heart backdoor. Pre-flop, A6s was a fine defend OOP multiway due to suited ace playability and pot odds, but it missed completely. Flash (SB) checking suggests weakness or trapping, but with Minni (BTN raiser, 2850 stack) behind, betting thin value/protection risks getting raised by real draws or fold equity wasted on calling stations. OOP pot control is key; checking realizes equity passively, induces bluffs from missed draws later, and keeps pot small with marginal hand. Deep stacks (mine/Flash) allow turn flexibility. Betting polarizes range too thin here - check standard.

Multiway, monotone diamond flop (7♦-5♦-T♦) is a bad board for my opened hand Q♠3♣ — I have no diamonds and only one overcard. With two callers my continuation-bet equity and fold equity are low, so barreling here is thin and likely to get called by any ten, pair, or a diamond draw. I want pot control and to use position to see a turn for free. Plan: check and reevaluate on the turn — if a non-diamond Q or otherwise harmless card pairs me I can fire for value; if a diamond or heavy action arrives I will give up (fold to large bets/raises). Versus small turn bets (≈≤1/3 pot) I can sometimes call and re-evaluate; versus larger sizing I’ll fold.

On this monotone board, I have top pair (Tens) and the second-nut flush draw (Queen of diamonds). After everyone checked the flop, the Deuce of spades on the turn is a complete blank. While my hand is strong, I am out of position against two players, one of whom (Elon) has a large stack and the other (Minni) is short-stacked. Checking again is a solid pot-control move that allows me to see how the turn action unfolds. If Elon or Minni bets, I have more than enough equity and showdown value to call, and if it checks through again, I get a free look at the river with a hand that likely still holds the lead.

Turn 2s is a low brick that doesn't complete the diamond flush draw but keeps the board scary with straight possibilities (e.g., 68, 89 still drawing or made). My Ah6h remains ace-high no pair/draw, pure bluff-catcher with a strong ace blocker. Flash's second check OOP suggests capping range or missed draws, unlikely trapping multiway deep. OOP in BB multiway pot of 900 with deep effective stacks (Minni shallow at 2850), pot control is paramount - betting risks raise from Minni's continuing range (draws/pairs) or thin value, while check induces bluffs from missed draws, realizes any thin equity passively, and keeps pot manageable for river flexibility. No reason to bluff here with positionally disadvantaged spot and players behind; checking is GTO-standard float continuation.

I have Q♠3♣ on a three-diamond, multiway board (7♦-5♦-T♦) and the turn is a 2♠. I have no pair and no diamond — essentially no showdown equity vs any diamond/paired hands. Both opponents checked flop and turn, so my bluff equity is very low and betting into two players with nothing is a losing line. Pot control and using position to see a free river is the best play: check and fold to meaningful aggression. I may call a very small bet (~≤1/3 pot ≈300) for price to see the river, but will fold to larger bets/raises. Checking preserves my stack and keeps options open for the river.

The board has three diamonds, making any diamond in an opponent's hand a flush. However, the flop and turn both checked through, which strongly suggests that no one currently holds a flush. I have top pair (Tens) with a Queen kicker, and I also hold the Queen of diamonds, which blocks the second-nut flush. Given the passive action so far, my top pair is likely the best hand. I will check to see if anyone tries to bluff at the pot or to get to a cheap showdown. If either opponent bets a reasonable amount, I have a clear call based on the strength of my pair and the unlikely nature of them having a flush given the prior betting rounds.

River Jc is a blank that completes some straight draws like 89 for 7-8-9-T-J and possibly backdoor stuff, but doesn't hit the diamond flush draw. My Ah6h is still pure ace-high bluff-catcher with a premium blocker to Ad/Kd/Qd nuts. Multiway checked to river pot of 900 with deep stacks except Minni (2850). Flash's third check OOP caps their range at weak pairs/missed draws, unlikely to have monster trapping this deep multiway. Betting thin value with ace-high risks Minni raising better (T7/set/pair+J/straight) or folding worse, while bluffs are poor OOP with Minni behind who can easily call/raise light due to position/shallow stack. Checking realizes equity passively, takes it down often vs air, and allows Minni to bluff into me if they missed (BTN wide range has many floats/draws). GTO prefers check-back here for pot control and protection in tough spot; no fancy play needed.

Board: 7♦-5♦-T♦-2♠-J♣. My hand Q♠3♣ has no pair, no diamond, and only queen-high. It’s a three-way hand where both opponents checked flop/turn — indicating missed draws or weak showdown holdings. Bluffing into two opponents with deep stacks has very low fold equity, and any value hands (pairs, T/J, or a made diamond) will call. Pot is only 900 and I’m ~28.5BB; preserving my stack and avoiding a multiway bluff is correct. I’ll check to take the free showdown and fold to meaningful aggression (I would only consider calling a very small bet ≤~1/3 pot).

I am on the button with 8s7c. This is a standard late-position opening hand in a three-handed game. My stack is healthy (195.75 BBs), and Minni, the big blind, is quite short-stacked with only 27.5 BBs. Raising here puts pressure on both blinds and allows me to take down the pot pre-flop or play a pot in position. I will raise to 300.

J3o is a trash hand with poor playability, no straight or flush potential, and dominated by any calling range. Facing a button raise, which is likely wide but still ahead of my hand. Out of position in SB, calling OOP is -EV due to difficult post-flop decisions. 3-betting as a bluff is suboptimal without better blockers or fold equity against both button and short-stacked BB who may shove. Pot odds (250 to call into 450) are insufficient given low equity and position disadvantage. Standard fold to preserve stack.

Facing a button open to 300 in 3-handed action with KcQc in the big blind (pot 450, to call 200). KQ suited is strong enough to 3-bet: great playability, useful blockers to AK/AQ, and we prefer to take the initiative since we’ll be OOP postflop. A standard ~3x 3-bet to 900 gives value vs a wide open range and good fold equity without committing too much of my 2750 stack (raising to 900 means I put in 800 more, leaving ~1950 behind). If Flash 4-bets very large I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold), but vs a call I have a hand that plays well postflop. I want to isolate the opener and build the pot with a strong, playable holding, so I 3-bet to 900.

I opened from the button with 8s7c, a standard stealing attempt. Minni, who is playing a very short stack of approximately 28.5 BBs total, has 3-bet to 900. This 3-bet represents nearly half of Minni's remaining stack, signifying they are pot-committed and likely holding a strong linear range (high pairs or strong Ace-X hands). While I am getting decent pot odds to call (600 to win a pot of 1250), 8s7c performs poorly against a short-stack's 3-betting range. Since I will likely have to face an all-in on almost any flop and my hand lacks sufficient equity or playability to justify continuing, folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my large stack.

Preflop on the button with AJo offsuit, a strong hand in position. Facing a small bet of 100 into a 150 pot from the SB (Minni with short stack of ~31BB), likely a weak raise or limp-raise. Raising builds the pot, has fold equity against BB (Flash deep but history shows aggression), and isolates the shorter-stacked SB. Standard sizing: raise to 4x the bet (400 total) for value and protection. Excellent play in position to realize equity.

I have pocket deuces (2♦2♥) in the small blind facing a button open to 400 in a three-handed pot. With ~31 BB effective this is a standard set‑mining spot — calling preserves implied odds if I hit a set (~11.8%) while a 3‑bet here would both reduce those implied odds and commit more with a hand that plays poorly multiway. Elon’s button open is likely reasonably wide and Flash may fold or call; calling lets me see a flop cheaply and keep options. Plan: call to see the flop, fold to a large 3‑bet preflop, and postflop play straightforwardly (value/poise if I hit, pot control or fold if I miss and face heavy action).

I have Ac 4c in the Big Blind. Elon raised to 400 from the Button, and Minni called from the Small Blind. I need to call 300 more into a pot of 900, giving me 4:1 pot odds. Ac 4c is a strong suited ace that plays well multi-way. While a squeeze is possible, calling closes the pre-flop action and allows me to see a flop with a hand that has good nut potential (flush) and decent high-card value in a three-handed game.

I have pocket deuces and missed my set on a dry K‑4‑3 rainbow flop in a three‑way pot. In multiway spots an underpair has very little bluffing or fold equity and is vulnerable to any K, A, or overpair — betting here will often commit me with the worst hand. I want to control the pot and realize my equity rather than build a pot I’m likely to lose. Plan: check and see a cheap turn. If I face a small probe bet (small fraction of the pot) I can call to keep alive for a backdoor set/river card; if I face a large/for‑value bet (half‑pot or larger) I’ll fold most of the time since my hand rarely holds. If the turn brings a deuce I’ll extract value; if it brings a king/ace or pairs the board and I face aggression I’ll fold. Checking keeps my options and preserves stack versus two deeper players who put pressure postflop.

On this K-4-3 rainbow flop, I have bottom pair with an Ace kicker. This is a marginal hand in a three-way pot. Minni, the small blind, has checked to me. Elon, the pre-flop aggressor, is still to act behind me. Betting here doesn't achieve much as I'm unlikely to fold out a King and I'd like to keep the pot small with my mediocre holding. Checking allows me to see how Elon reacts to the board and keeps my options open for a call if he bets a reasonable amount.

Flop checked to me on the button as preflop raisor. Board is Kd4s3h: relatively dry with king high, possible spade flush draw and wheel straight draws, but my AJo blocks strong Ax hands like AK/AQ and has ace-high showdown value. Opponents' calling ranges preflop likely include suited connectors, broadway, pairs but many miss this board. C-bet for value against weaker kings/pairs/draws, fold equity vs air, and to deny equity to draws. Minni short-stacked (2800) may over-shove monsters but folds medium strength; Flash deep but history shows folds to aggression. Sizing 60% pot (700) balances value/protection/fold equity while keeping pot manageable in position.

Call. Elon leads 700 into a 1900 pot (~37% sizing) on K♦‑4♠‑3♥; I have pocket 2s — an underpair in a three‑way pot. This is a set‑mining spot: I have ~11.8% to hit a set by the river and strong implied odds against two deeper stacks if I do. Folding to a small probe here is too tight; raising would be thin and would reduce my implied odds while committing with a hand that plays poorly multi‑way. Calling keeps the pot controlled, realizes equity, and lets me reevaluate on the turn. Plan after the call: if I hit a deuce I extract value; if the turn is a blank and I face significant aggression (a half‑pot+ bet or a raise) I will usually fold; if action checks through I can go to showdown or consider a small value bet on favorable cards. If Flash raises right now I will fold.

On a board of Kd-4s-3h, I have bottom pair with an Ace kicker. While I have a piece of the board, Elon's bet into two players and Minni's call change the situation significantly. Minni is playing a very short stack (approx. 10 big blinds remaining after the call), which suggests their range for calling is quite narrow and likely consists of a King, a better 4, or a straight draw like 56. Sandwiched between a bet and a call in a multi-way pot, my bottom pair is very vulnerable. Although the pot odds are tempting (roughly 4.7:1), the likelihood of being ahead or having enough clean outs to improve is low. I will fold to preserve my stack and look for a better spot.

I have pocket deuces (2♦2♥) on K♦-4♠-3♥-9♥ after calling Elon’s 700 lead on the flop. I missed my set; only two outs to hit on the river (~4.3%), the pot is 2600 and my stack is 2100. This is a classic pot-control situation: as an underpair heads‑up versus the preflop/turn aggressor I have very little fold equity and betting or raising would often commit me with the worst hand. I will check to realize equity and keep the pot small. Plan after checking: if Elon fires a tiny probe (only a very small bet — roughly up to ~100–150 chips given my set‑odds) I may call to see the river; if he bets a meaningful amount (roughly half‑pot or larger, ≈1300+) or raises, I will fold most of the time. If he checks back I’ll evaluate the river and either show down or fold to strong action.

Turn board brings the 9h, adding another heart to the board (now two hearts: 3h, 9h), strengthening heart flush draws and possibly completing some. Possible straight draws like 2-5 wheel still live but marginal. My AhJc is still just ace-high showdown value, blocking some AxK/AQ and nut flush draws, but facing Minni's flop call from SB (short stack now ~2100 into 2600 pot), her range is weighted toward Kx (KQ, KJ, etc., which beat me), pairs that improved (two-pair/set), or strong draws that hit or semi-bluff called. As preflop raiser and flop c-bettor, my range is perceived strong (KK+, AK, sets), but I don't beat much of her calling range. Betting risks getting shoved on by better (Kx) or drawing to nuts while charging draws incorrectly; thin value bets don't work well vs short stack who can overcommit.
