Checking back preserves stack, realizes equity with ace-high (blocks her strong Ax), controls pot, and gets to showdown cheaply in position. Free river card denies her equity if bluffing/checking back weak kings/draws. Optimal with marginal hand on scary board.

I check. I have pocket deuces and missed my set; the river K (board 4♠‑K♦‑3♥‑9♥‑K♥) pairs the board and gives many hands in Elon’s line (open, flop c‑bet, then check on the 9♥ turn) top pair or better. My hand only beats rare bluffs or very light air — betting here has very little fold equity and almost no value. With the pot at 2600 and my stack 2100 I don't want to commit chips with a hand that will rarely be best; checking and taking it to showdown preserves my stack. If Elon then fires a meaningful river bet I will fold.

River pairs the kings (now Kd Kh) and adds a third heart (3h 9h Kh), putting flushes in play for opponents holding exactly two hearts. My AhJc provides pair of kings with premium kickers (A J 9 vs board's 9 4 3), ace-high showdown value blocking AK/AQ, but critically loses to any Kx (trips kings or better), which is heavily weighted in Minni's OOP flop-calling range after defending SB vs BTN open and calling 700 into ~2100 pot on Kd-high dry board. Her turn/river check-check after flop call suggests not the nuts (sets, two-pair KK/full house unlikely) but likely mid-Kx (KJ/KT/K9), stubborn pairs/draws, or now-flushed hands avoiding a bet. I checked turn, capping my range somewhat, but position allows cheap showdown. Value betting risks her calling profitably with better (trips) or raising all-in (2100 into 2600, she gets great odds); thin value vs worse kicker trips impossible since trips always beat pair. Bluffing poor as her range showdowns strong. Checking realizes equity vs her possible no-king hands (Axo, QQ-JJ now two-pair but unlikely callers, missed draws), controls pot OOP, wins vs air/bluffs/medium kickers without showdown, and preserves deep stack for better spots late in session. Optimal passive line with marginal strength on paired scary board.

Button with K♢J♠ in a 3-handed pot — this is a clear open. KJ offsuit is strong in position and we should take the initiative to extract fold equity and play postflop last. Stack depths (~47BB) allow a standard raise without committing too much. I’ll open to 3x the big blind (300 total) to apply pressure to the blinds, avoid the passive limp line, and keep a playable pot if called. Plan: if called, use position to c-bet favorable flops and realize equity; if 3-bet large, reevaluate vs opponent and likely fold to very large 3-bets absent a clear read.

I am holding 2c 7c in the Small Blind. Minni has raised to 3x the big blind from the button. While the hand is suited, it is extremely weak (bottom of the range) and playing out of position against a button raise is generally unprofitable. Furthermore, Minni's relatively short stack (44bb) makes it harder to realize equity if I were to hit a draw. Folding is the most prudent play.

Preflop in BB facing Button minraise to 300 after SB fold. Pot 450, calling 200 into it gives excellent implied odds (over 2:1). T5h suited connectors have good playability: straight draws, flush potential, and blocks broadway. Button range is wide (plays many hands aggressively per history). Effective stacks shallow for Minni (4400), but SPR post-call ~6x heads-up, suitable for speculative hand. Folding too tight; 3-betting marginal as not strong enough vs wide range. Call to realize equity and play postflop OOP with position advantage heads-up.

Flop action first to act OOP with top pair (Tens, Td paired by Th) + strong nut flush draw (5h, board 7h 3h Td; 9 flush outs clean). Board draw heavy (hearts, 4-5-6 str8 draw, 8-9 str8 draw). Minni's button minraise range wide: suited Ax (heart draws), broadway, suited connectors, weaker pairs/A-high. Excellent equity vs range (~60%+). Bet for thin value (vs worse pairs/Ax), protection (charge draws), build pot low SPR (her 4400 stack). Check risks free card to improve draws or checkraise; leading extracts value now. Size 2/3 pot (450) balances value/protection, induces calls from worse, sets up turn play.

Elon (BB) called preflop and now bets 450 into a 1,100 pot on 7♥ T♦ 3♥. With K♦J♠ I have two clean overcards and ~24% chance to pair by the river. Calling 450 to potentially win ~2,000 (1100+450+450) requires ~22.5% equity, so a call is +EV. I have position and can realize equity and control pot size—raising to 900 is a viable semi-bluff but Elon shows aggression and is likely to call/3-bet with many made hands, which would bloat the pot with no pair. Plan: call to keep worse hands/bluffs in and reassess on the turn—bet for value if I pair K or J, fold or pot-control if the turn brings a scary heart or a better board for his range, and exploit any turn check-backs.

On the turn, the 6c is a relatively blank card for most ranges, completing some gutshots (like 4-5) but not changing the board texture drastically—no flush completes, no obvious straight for Minni's wide button calling range. I have top pair (Th pairing Td) with a strong flush draw (9 clean heart outs) and a gutshot to the wheel straight (3 outs to 4x, some overlapping with flush). Equity remains very high (~60-70% vs. her flop calling range: worse pairs like 88-99/77/33/Ax suited, heart draws like KhQh/Jh9h/8h6h, straight draws like 89/45/56). Pot is 1550, her stack 3950 (SPR ~2.5), mine deep. OOP, betting extracts value from worse made hands/draws, denies equity to pure draws, and sets up river for thin value or getting it in if she shoves. Checking risks giving a free card to draws or facing a check-raise, and pot control is less necessary with such a polarized strong hand. Bet sizing: ~2/3 pot (1000) balances value (beats worse pairs/draws) and protection, leaves her ~2950 behind into ~3550 pot (good for calls/shoves). History shows Minni calls bets with marginal holdings/draws (e.g., Hand 1, 4, etc.), so likely to continue. Maximizes EV by building pot with nuts-ish equity.

Fold. Elon’s turn raise to 1,000 into a 2,550 pot gives me terrible pot odds — I must have roughly 28% equity to justify a call (1,000/(2,550+1,000) ≈ 28.2%). With K♦J♠ I only have 6 clean outs (3 kings + 3 jacks) ≈ 13% to pair by the river and no backdoor heart/straight to meaningfully increase equity. Elon’s line (call preflop, bet the flop, big turn bet) is very value‑heavy here (tens, sevens, sets, strong heart/Ax combos) and he’s deep enough to apply pressure; a bluff‑raise would be high risk and likely called. Calling would commit ~25% of my stack with far too little equity, so I fold and preserve chips to pick better spots in position.

On the button with Ks 9s, this is a strong opening hand in a three-handed game. My stack is very healthy (188.25 BBs). Elon and Minni have both been active, but K9 suited is well within the top range for a button raise. I want to build the pot and take control of the hand pre-flop. Standard raise to 3x big blind is 300.

Q3o is a trash hand: offsuit, disconnected, dominated by any queen or better in Flash's button raising range, which is wide but still strong. OOP in SB, post-flop playability is poor. Pot odds to call 250 into 450 aren't compelling given low equity (~20-30% vs typical button open). BB might squeeze or call, complicating further. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium hands in 126/150 hand marathon. Focus on maximizing EV long-term.

I’m on the BB (100 posted) with QdJd facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and it costs me 200 more to call (I’ll have 300 committed total). Stacks ~38.5BB — deep enough to play postflop. QJ suited is a strong hand but out of position; it has good equity vs a wide button opening range and the pot odds (450:200 ≈ 2.25:1) make a call +EV. I prefer to call here rather than 3‑bet: a 3‑bet commits more stack OOP and will often be met with calls or 4‑bets from a big‑stack, while calling lets me realize equity, play flops with draws/top pairs, and keep weaker hands and bluffs in range. Plan after the call: c‑bet/donk when I have strong draws or pair, fold to heavy, committed aggression on very dry boards if I miss, and extract value when I hit. Therefore I’ll call the 200 (300 total committed).

I missed this flop — QdJd has no pair and only a remote backdoor diamond (one diamond on a 9♦ 3♠ 3♣ board). Out of position vs a button opener and facing a paired, relatively dry flop, I want to keep the pot small and realize equity rather than bluff‑bloat. Checking controls the pot, lets me see a turn for a pair or diamond, and avoids committing chips with a marginal hand. Plan: check-call a small continuation bet to see the turn, fold to significant aggression (big raise/shove), and extract value or bluff when I actually pick up a pair or a strong diamond draw.

I have top pair with a King kicker on a paired board (9-3-3). This is a very strong hand three-handed. Minni checked, which is expected as the defender. I should bet here to get value from worse pairs (like 88-44), Ace-high hands that might peel, or draws. Given the small pot (650), a half-pot bet is a standard continuation to keep the range wide and extract value.

I checked the flop and Flash c‑bet 325 into a 650 pot. Calling 325 to win 975 gives ~3:1 pot odds (≈25% required equity). Q♦J♦ has roughly ~24–28% realistic equity vs a wide button opening range on this dry, paired 9♦-3♠-3♣ flop (pairing by river + small runner‑runner diamond/further improvements). Flash is an aggressive button who c‑bets these boards frequently, so his range is likely wide enough that a call to realize equity is +EV. A raise OOP with no pair and only backdoor equity would be thin and high variance; folding now surrenders too much equity. I’ll call to see the turn (300 preflop committed + 325 now = 625 total committed). Plan on the turn: continue/double-barrel only with a real improvement (pair, single‑card diamond draw, or specific read), otherwise fold to heavy turn aggression.

Pot 1300, my stack ~3325. I hold Q♦J♦ and I called the flop c-bet to realize equity vs Flash's wide button c-betting range. The turn (T♣) gives me no pair and only a backdoor double-runner diamond possibility — I have essentially no real draw and I'm out of position. Betting OOP here would be a thin bluff that bloats the pot against a deep, aggressive opponent who will often call or raise; I don't want to commit more chips with a marginal hand. Checking controls pot size, lets me realize showdown equity against many of his missed hands, and allows him to bluff or show weakness first. Plan: check and evaluate his turn action. If he checks back I see a free river; if he fires a small bet (roughly <= ~1/3 pot ≈ 400) I'll call to see the river; if he bets large (> ~1/2 pot) or raises I'll fold unless I pick up a clear improvement or read.

On the turn, the Ten of Clubs is an overcard to my pair of Nines. Minni has checked again after calling my flop continuation bet. While I still have a strong hand (top pair on the flop), the Ten could have improved a portion of her calling range (hands like AT, KT, QT, JT). Checking back here is a prudent move to control the pot size and realize my equity. It also allows me to induce bluffs on the river or reach a cheap showdown with a hand that has significant value but is vulnerable to a check-raise on this specific board texture.

I have Q♦J♦ and the final board 9♦ 3♠ 3♣ T♣ 8♦ — that gives me an 8–9–10–J–Q straight on the river. Against a wide BTN raise/c-bet range this hand is very often best: only full houses/quads (very specific combos like 9x+3x or pocket 3s/9s) or an unlikely higher straight could beat me. Flash’s line (open to 300, 325 flop c-bet, then check turn) looks exactly like a wide continuation-range that includes many single-pair hands (9x, Tx, smaller pairs), two-pair combos (T9) and bluffs — many of which will call a reasonable river value bet. I should extract value rather than check and allow him to realize equity for free. Size: roughly half pot (~650) is large enough to get calls from single pairs and two-pair combos but small enough that he will call with marginal holdings; it also keeps the SPR reasonable against his deep stack. Plan: bet ~650 for value; if he raises very big on the river I’ll assume a full house/quads and re-evaluate (likely fold to an overbet), but I want to collect thin value now.

I have a pair of Nines with a King kicker on a board of 9-3-3-10-8. I was the pre-flop aggressor and bet the flop when I had top pair. I checked the turn after a Ten appeared, which is an overcard to my Nine. Minni has led out on the river for 650 into a 1300 pot. This 'donk' bet on the river after the turn went check-check could indicate a marginal hand trying to get value (like a Ten or an Eight) or a bluff. Given that I have a King kicker and have shown weakness by checking the turn, I am high enough in my range to call here, especially getting 3:1 odds against a short-stacked opponent. I don't see enough value in raising, as she is likely to only call a raise with hands that beat me (a Three or a Ten).

Preflop on the button with 7s4s, a suited connector/gapper with good playability. Pot is 150, to call 100 gives immediate odds of 1.5:1, improving to about 2.5:1 after calling. Position is excellent, allowing control postflop. Deep stacks support implied odds for hitting sets, straights, flushes, or draws. Likely SB limp and BB small raise scenario; calling keeps pot small multiway if SB calls, or heads-up vs BB. Raising risks isolation with marginal hand or bloating pot OOP for SB; folding too tight given position and odds. Standard play: call to realize equity flexibly.

Fold. Holding J♠7♥ in the small blind against a limp from the button plus the big blind, this is a marginal, easily-dominated hand that plays very poorly out of position in a multiway pot. Calling 50 for price is cheap (pot becomes 300 for a 50 call), but my realized equity OOP against two callers is low and I’ll have tough, exploitative postflop decisions. A min-raise to 200 to steal is also unattractive — I’m unlikely to fold out both opponents and will often face calls or 3-bets from stronger ranges. With ~52BB and plenty of hands left, preserving my stack and waiting for a better, positionally favorable spot is higher EV than playing this one out.

In the Big Blind with K4o, Elon (the button) has limped into the pot for 100 total. Minni has folded from the Small Blind. I have a choice to check or raise. K4o is a marginal hand that plays poorly in raised pots, but I am in position relative to the post-flop action (even though I am BB, Elon is the Button). Given the pot is small (250) and Elon limped, there is little incentive to build a large pot with a weak King. Checking allows me to see a flop for free and realize equity without risking more chips.

On a flop of 9h Kh Qs, I have top pair with a weak kicker (Ks 4d). The board is quite coordinated, containing both straight and flush draws (JT for the straight, two hearts for the flush). Elon, who limped from the button, has checked to me. While my kicker is weak, I likely have the best hand here. I will lead with a small bet to get value from draws (like hearts or a Jack/Ten) and middle pairs (like a Queen or a Nine), and to protect my hand from being outdrawn on various turn cards. A bet of around half the pot is standard here.

Preflop limp with 7s4s suited gapper from button for position and implied odds, pot controlled multiway or heads-up. Flop 9h-Kh-Qs completely misses: no pair, no flush draw (only three spades total: my two + Qs), no straight draw (74 doesn't connect to KQ9; no gutshot or open-ender), pure air with zero showdown value. Flash donk-bets 125 into 375 pot (~1/3 pot) after checking preflop behind limp, which ranges wide: weak pairs, draws (hearts flush draw possible with two on board), Kx, Qx, air bluffs, or monsters slowplayed. Calling commits to awkward spot OOP on turn with no improved equity; raising as pure bluff risks spew vs calling station or strong hand, poor given deep stacks and position. Opponent history shows Flash mixes bets/checks but exploits position postflop. Best play: fold immediately to realize 0 equity realization and preserve stack for better spots. Goal is long-term stack maximization over 150 hands.

In 3-handed play the button has a wide opening range and A♣8♠ is a clear open — decent ace with position and ~52BB behind. Table norms show standard opens around 3x the BB, so I’ll raise to 300 to get fold equity vs the blinds, build the pot when I have position, and avoid giving opponents a free flop. Post-flop plan: continuation-bet many flops (especially A-high or dry boards) and proceed in position; if I face a large 3-bet I’ll fold to heavy aggression but will consider calling small 3-bets to realize equity in position.

I am holding 7c 6c in the small blind. Minni, on the button, has raised to 300. With a stack of nearly 50 big blinds, Minni is relatively short compared to the table average. 76s is a strong suited connector that performs well in multi-way pots and has good playability post-flop. Calling the 250 additional chips provides me with decent pot odds to see a flop, even though I will be out of position for the rest of the hand. I expect the Big Blind (Elon) to call or fold frequently here, allowing us to see a flop with a hand that can flop strong draws or disguised pairs.

Preflop in the big blind with 4c7d, a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity. Facing a button raise from Minni (short stack at ~49BB) and a small blind call from Flash (deep stack). Pot odds are about 3.5:1 (200 to call into 700), but this hand performs poorly multiway, has no blockers, no suitedness, and minimal straight potential. Standard fold from BB defense range against late position raise and caller behind. Preserving stack for better spots in late game (hand 128/150).

On this flop of 2c Qs 5c, I have flopped a flush draw with my 7c 6c. Being out of position as the pre-flop caller, the standard play is to check to the aggressor (Minni) to see how she reacts to this board. Given her relatively short stack of approximately 50 big blinds, I want to keep the pot manageable while I have high equity but haven't made my hand yet. If she bets, I have a very easy call or potentially a check-raise depending on her sizing and my read on her range, but for now, checking is the most sound tactical approach.

I opened from the button and have position; Flash (SB) just called and checked the flop. Board 2c-Qs-5c is relatively dry except for two clubs and a high queen — my A♣8♠ has the Ace blocker and only Ace-high plus a backdoor club runner, so it’s a marginal hand but ideal for a continuation bet to take the pot down or charge weaker holdings. I’ll c-bet about half pot (350): big enough to fold out small pairs, weak Qx and many overcards/draws, but small enough to keep the pot manageable given I have no pair. If I face a strong check-raise I’ll fold (I’m only on Ace-high); if called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn — continue on dry turns and proceed cautiously if the queen pairs or heavy aggression arrives.

I have flopped a flush draw with 7c 6c on a 2c Qs 5c board. The pot is 1050 and Minni has bet 350, which is exactly 1/3 of the current pot size. This gives me pot odds of 3:1 (requiring 25% equity to call). With nine clubs remaining in the deck, I have approximately a 19% chance of hitting the flush on the turn and about 35% by the river. Adding in some minor equity from potential 7s or 6s (though they may not be the best hand if they hit) and a gutshot straight draw if a 4 or 8 appears, calling is very profitable from a mathematical standpoint. Since Minni is playing a relatively short stack (approximately 45 big blinds), I want to realize my equity without bloating the pot too much while out of position, so I will call the bet and evaluate the turn.

On the turn Jack of Diamonds, the board texture has changed slightly, but it doesn't improve my 7-high flush draw. Being out of position against the pre-flop and flop aggressor (Minni), the most standard and effective play is to check. This allows me to see how she values her hand on this new street. If she bets, I will evaluate the pot odds offered to continue chasing my flush, especially considering she has a relatively short stack remaining. Checking also allows me to potentially see a free river if she decides to check back with a marginal hand or a draw herself.

Board is 2c Qs 5c Jd, pot 1,400. I have Ac8s — Ace-high with the club blocker. Flash check-called my small flop c-bet, so his range is wide: Qx, middle pocket pairs, some club draws and float/call hands. The turn Jd is a relatively dry card and didn’t pair the queen or add a scary club; per my flop plan I should continue on dry turns to charge draws and extract value from worse Kx/Ax and some weaker pairs/floats. I’ll bet ~50% pot (700). Size is big enough to deny free cards and price out some draws, but small enough to keep the pot manageable with only Ace-high. If I face a large raise I will give it up (I’m not committed with only Ace-high); if called I’ll re-evaluate on the river based on the card and opponent action.

On the turn, the Jack of Diamonds has been dealt, and I am still holding a 7-high flush draw. The pot is 1400 and Minni has bet 700, which gives me pot odds of 3:1 (requiring 25% equity to call). With 9 outs to a flush, my direct equity to hit on the river is approximately 19.5%. While this is slightly below the 25% threshold, the implied odds are favorable as Minni has around 3875 chips remaining. If I hit the flush on the river, I can likely extract further value from her range, which likely includes Queens, Jacks, or other made hands she is choosing to barrel. Therefore, calling the 700 is a mathematically sound decision based on potential payoff.

I am holding 7c 6c and have missed my flush draw and gutshot on the river. The board has paired with a second Deuce (2c Qs 5c Jd 2s). I have no showdown value with 7-high. Minni has been the aggressor throughout the hand, raising pre-flop and betting both the flop and the turn. Given her relatively short stack (approximately 38 big blinds remaining) and the aggression she has shown, it is highly likely she holds a made hand like a Queen or a Jack. Since she is likely pot-committed with any pair, a bluff is unlikely to succeed. I will check and hope she checks back with a hand I can somehow beat (unlikely) or simply fold if she bets, as I cannot justify a call with 7-high.

Flash called both my flop c-bet and my turn bet, which indicates a fairly strong/calling range (single-pair Qx, Jx, middle pocket pairs, some made two-pair/trips combos if he held a deuce). The river 2s pairs the board and improves many of his continuing hands; I only have Ace-high (Ac8s) — the Ac is a small blocker but I have essentially no value. The pot is 2,800 and my stack is 3,875, so any meaningful bluff risks a large portion of my stack versus a caller-heavy range. A river bluff here has thin fold equity and poor risk/reward, so I’ll pot-control and check to showdown.
I have KTs on the button, which is a strong starting hand in a 3-handed game. My stack is healthy at 163.5 big blinds. I will raise to 300 to build the pot and take control of the hand in position.

95o is a complete trash hand with no high card strength, poor connectivity, and offsuit. Facing a button raise from Flash, who often opens wide but still has a strong enough range to dominate this. I'm OOP in the SB, which amplifies the hand's poor playability post-flop. Pot odds are ~2:1 but equity multiway (with BB yet to act) is insufficient, and speculative hands like this bleed money long-term. Folding preserves stack for better spots in late game (hand 129/150).

I’m in the big blind with Q♦5♥ facing a button open to 300. The pot is 450 and I must call 200 more, which requires roughly 200/(450+200) ≈ 30.8% equity to break even. Q5o is a weak, frequently dominated holding vs a button opening range (many Qx and broadways) and has poor post‑flop playability out of position. A 3‑bet bluff isn’t attractive here — villain is deep-stacked and will call or 4‑bet enough to make this risky with little fold equity. Preserving my ~65bb stack and folding to this open is the +EV play.

3-handed on the button, first to act preflop with 63s suited connectors. Deep stacks overall (169BB, opponents 65BB and 164BB). Button RFI range is very wide 3-handed (~50%+ hands), and 63s has excellent playability with straight/flush potential, suited, and position for postflop control. Standard open to 3x BB (300) to build pot, take initiative, and realize equity multiway if called. Limping weak, folding too tight. History shows frequent 300 opens by all, fits pattern. Minni shorter stack may defend wide.

I have TT in the small blind facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, 250 to call). With ~65bb effective this is a standard 3-bet spot for value/isolation. Calling 250 keeps me OOP and often invites a multiway pot against Flash; a tiny raise (to 500) gives little fold equity. I want a size that (a) folds out some of the big blind, (b) gives good fold equity vs Elon’s opening range, and (c) keeps SPR reasonable postflop. I will 3-bet to 1000 total — large enough to discourage the BB and extract value from worse broadways/flush draws, while preserving flexibility if Elon 4-bets (I can fold or re-evaluate vs a large 4-bet). If called, pot ≈ 2100 and my remaining stack (~5525) gives a low SPR where TT plays well.

I am in the big blind with Queen-Seven offsuit. Elon (button) raised to 300, and Minni (small blind) 3-bet to 1000. Minni is relatively short-stacked (~55bb) compared to the rest of the table. My hand is weak and does not play well in a multi-way or 3-bet pot out of position. Given the action, Minni's 3-bet range should be quite strong, and Elon's opening range is wide. However, with a 3-bet already on the table and me being out of position, the best course of action is to fold and wait for a better spot.

Preflop on the button with 63s suited connectors facing a 3-bet to 1000 from Minni in the SB (55BB effective). Pot 1400, calling 700 more (total 1000 committed), offering ~2:1 odds. 63s plays well multiway or heads-up with position: high playability, straight/flush potential, dominates worse suited connectors/gappers in her range. 3-handed SB 3-bets are often polarized (value QQ+/AK or bluffs/light like Axs, KQs, suited connectors). Equity ~35% vs typical range, plus implied odds on deep stacks (my 166BB). Folding too tight from button; 4-betting risks bloating pot OOP with speculative hand. Calling realizes equity in position, controls pot postflop, fits aggressive game dynamics from history where Minni 3-bets wide/shoves light. Maximizes EV long-term.

I check. On A♣ J♦ 5♠ rainbow I have middle pair (TT) out of position in a 3‑bet pot. Elon's cold‑call range from the button contains a lot of A‑x, some Jx and broadway hands plus small/medium pairs — the Ace on the flop hits his range hard. A flop continuation bet here has thin value (many hands that will call beat me or are too close) and poor fold equity, while bloating the pot OOP with a marginal holding. Pot control and information are preferable: check to induce bluffs, allow him to barrel missed hands, and only commit to small bets where I can call and re‑evaluate. Plan: check‑call small/medium bets to see a cheap turn; fold to large polarized bets that commit too much with just middle pair; consider a delayed bet only on safe runouts or when I get a clear read of weakness.

Postflop on Ac Jd 5s board, heads-up vs Minni's SB 3bet range after she checks. My 63s gives open-ended straight draw (8 outs: 2,4,7; ~32% equity standalone), backdoor spade flush draw, excellent playability in position with deep effective stacks (Minni ~55BB). Her check polarizes range: weak Ax bluffs, missed broadways, or slowplayed AA/AK/JJ. Board somewhat coordinated but A-top favors my wide BTN calling range slightly. Betting exploits her weak check-back range, denies equity to floats/draws, builds pot for implied odds when we hit (e.g., turn 2/4/7 gives str8), protects vs her better hands. Check-back risks giving free card to her OESD/gutshots. Standard 2/3 pot bet (1400) sizes well: small enough for calls from worse (Ax, Jx, sets drawing thin), large enough to fold out bluffs/floats, pot commitment pressure on her shorter stack. History shows aggressive lines, fits maximizing EV in 3-handed dynamic.

I 3-bet preflop to isolate and checked the Ace‑high flop to control the pot with middle pair (TT). Elon, who cold‑called the 3‑bet, bets 1400 into ~2100 (~67% pot). Folding here is too weak against his wide button‑calling range because many of his continuation‑bet hands (KQ/Kx, medium pairs, missed broadways) I beat, and the pot odds to call (1400 to win ~3500 → ~29% equity needed) are reasonable. Raising OOP is thin and will put me in difficult spots versus Ax/Jx (which rarely fold) and unnecessarily commits my stack. I’ll call 1400 to realize equity, keep the pot controllable, and re‑evaluate on the turn: call small/medium barrels and fold to large polarized turn bets (especially if an A or J lands), consider a delayed bluff only on very favorable runouts.

I have TT (second pair) in a 3‑bet pot. Preflop I 3‑bet to 1000 and called Elon's large flop C‑bet (1400) — pot is now ~4900 and my effective stack is ~4175. The turn pairs the board with a 5 (5s/5h) and Elon checked to me. His check polarizes his line toward pot control with Ax or giving up with missed broadways; many of the hands that continued on the flop (Ax, Jx) still beat my tens. Betting here has very thin value — it will rarely fold out Ax/Jx (the hands that beat me) and will mainly get called by worse hands or get me raised into a committing spot. With no significant draws to protect against and a paired board that reduces blockers to sets, the prudent line is pot control: check back the turn, see the river, and call reasonable river bets while folding to large polarized river aggression. Checking preserves stack, keeps bluffs in Elon's range, and lets me make a cleaner decision on the river.

Turn board pairs the 5s (5s Jd Ac 5h), improving the board's coordination slightly but capping top end at trips/Ax while adding straight draw possibilities (e.g., 67). Holding 63s suited on button OOP vs Minni's SB 3-bet range: nut spade flush draw (9 outs, ~19% equity heads-up), gutshot to 7 for straight (~4% additional), total ~25%+ equity vs her range. Position, initiative from flop c-bet, and her double-check (flop check-call, turn check) polarizes her: likely medium Ax (AJ, A5s, AQo+), slowplayed sets/two-pair, or missed draws she check-calls wide (history shows Minni defends light, calls big bets). Deep effective (my 145BB, her ~42BB left into 49BB pot). Betting exploits: gets value from Ax/Jx worse flush draws, protects equity vs her improving hands/river draws, max fold equity on bluffs/floats given pot commitment (call would leave her ~1.5BB). Check risks free river to her outs (e.g., J, A improves her). Semi-bluff bet ~2/3 pot (3200) sizes well: thin value from Ax, folds out pure bluffs/marginal calls, near pot shove pressure on short stack without overbetting spewily. Fits aggressive history (e.g., Hands 83,87 big bets payoff), 3-handed dynamic where position + draws dominate. EV+ long-term stack maximization.

Pot is 8,100 and Elon fires a large turn bet of 3,200. I hold TTs on A♣ J♦ 5♠ 5♥ with 4,175 behind. Preflop I 3‑bet to isolate and called his 1,400 flop C‑bet to control the pot and realize equity. The turn pairs the board and his line (cold‑call 3‑bet, C‑bet the A‑J‑5, now a big turn barrel) is heavily weighted toward value: A‑x, J‑x, sets (55) and A5 two‑pair are all very plausible and beat my tens. There are very few believable bluffs or semi‑bluffs here. To call I need roughly 3,200 / (8,100+3,200) ≈ 28% equity; against a value‑heavy continuing range TT is well under that and calling would leave me with only ~975 behind and force a marginal river decision. Jamming (raising to 4,175) is essentially an all‑in bluff that will get called by most Ax/sets and is not +EV. Given the line, sizing and my previous plan to fold to large polarized turn aggression with middle pair, the correct long‑term play is to fold and preserve my stack for better spots.

Pocket fives on the button with ~41.8 BB effective — this is a classic set-mining spot. I’ll call 100 to see the flop: it’s the cheapest way to realize my strong implied odds versus two deep, calling opponents while keeping the pot manageable. Raising to isolate is an option but would bloat the pot and invite 3-bets from these aggressive, deep stacks. Plan: use position postflop — fold to heavy action if I completely miss, and extract value when I hit a set or a favorable low board.

I am in the Small Blind with T2o. The Button (Minni) has limped in, and I only have to call 50 more chips to see a flop. However, T2o is a very weak hand. Even with the attractive pot odds (250 pot, 50 to call), this hand plays poorly post-flop, especially out of position against a player who has shown aggression in previous hands. Minni's stack is also relatively short (around 40 BBs), making it less ideal to play speculative trash. Given the marginal nature of the hand, folding is the most disciplined play to avoid losing more chips on later streets.

Preflop in BB with 3h4d, a trash hand (34o) with no high card value, poor connectivity for suitedness is missing, and terrible playability OOP. Minni limped from button (wide range likely), SB Flash folded. Pot 250, can check for free to realize any equity on flop cheaply. Raising has no value and high risk OOP with this hand; better to check and fold to aggression or play postflop if improved. Deep stacks but Minni shorter at 4075; preserve chips for premium spots as game nears end (hand 131/150).

Flop is 8d As 6d. Holding 3d4h: complete air - no pair, no straight draw (gutshot at best to 5 but unlikely), backdoor diamond draw at most with one diamond. Minni limped BTN (wide range: pairs, suited connectors, Ax, broadways, flush draws). Ace-high board hits her range strongly; two-tone diamonds favor her limping suited hands. OOP first to act in BB vs shorter-stacked aggressive Minni (4075 chips). Betting has zero value (no showdown, no fold equity with pure bluff), risks stack-off to her value. Check-fold optimal: realize 0 equity cheaply, preserve deep stack (19325) for 20 remaining hands where premiums matter. No pot control needed with nothing.

I called preflop as a set-mining play and completely missed this A♠ 8♦ 6♦ flop. I’m heads-up with deep stacks so pot control and information are priority — betting an underpair on an Ace-high board is value-destroying and will often get me turned into by stronger hands. I’ll check to keep the pot small, see how Elon reacts, and only commit further if I pick up improvement or face a small probe bet (call small bets to see a turn; fold to heavy aggression). If I hit a 5 or the board becomes favorable on the turn I’ll shift to value extraction.

Turn board 8d As 6d 3h pairs my 3d for bottom pair with 4 kicker, on an ace-high flop with diamond flush draw and potential straight draws (5-7, 4-5-7 etc.). Heads-up OOP vs Minni's button limp range (wide: Ax, suited connectors, pairs 22-JJ, diamonds). She checked flop, which could be weak ace, missed draw, or trap with strong hand. Pot tiny at 250, effective stacks Minni 4075 (shorter). Betting thin value risks raising her strong hands or getting called by better; no fold equity needed as pot small. Check preserves stack (19325 deep, 19 hands left), controls pot, realizes equity cheaply, induces bluffs from her missed draws (e.g., flush/straight draws whiffed), or allows fold to aggression if she values. Optimal OOP with marginal bottom pair on scary board late in session.

Board A♠ 8♦ 6♦ 3♥, I hold 5♠5♥. This is an underpair to the Ace on a two-diamond board with essentially zero fold equity to a large reaction and only two outs (two remaining 5s) to make a set (~4.3% to hit the river). Elon checked flop and again on the turn from the big blind, which suggests a wide, often weak or medium-strength checking range (missed overcards, small pairs, or some draws). Betting now would bloat the pot and put me in difficult spots vs A-x, sets, or diamond draws; a small probe could pick up the pot but is thinly profitable and will be punished when called. I’ll check to control the pot and see the river for free. Plan: if the river pairs me (5) I value-bet; if the river misses I’ll check-fold to a meaningful bet and only call very small bluffs. Checking preserves stack and keeps live outs intact.

River 6h pairs the board's 6d, giving everyone at least pair of 6s. However, holding 3d4h pairs the board's turn 3h, resulting in two pair (6s and 3s) with A kicker. This is a strong hand heads-up vs Minni's button limp range, which checked flop (Ad heavy) and turn without aggression, suggesting missed draws (e.g., diamond flush/straight draws like 57d, 47d, 79d), weak pairs (77,55,44), or trapped medium strength (AJo, KQo unlikely given checks). Beats most of her calling range (worse two pair unlikely without 3x, single pair 6s with poor kicker, air). Pot tiny at 250, effective stack Minni 4075 (shorter). OOP first to act on river after check-check streets; small value bet extracts from worse (pair 6s, missed draws) while risking little relative to deep stack (19325) and endgame (131/150). Larger bet risks fold; 150 ~60% pot induces calls without scaring off value. Check risks free showdown or her check-behind with worse/equal; betting maximizes EV late.

Board: A♠ 8♦ 6♦ 3♥ 6♥. I hold 5♠5♥ — an underpair to the Ace with no improvement. Elon checked flop and turn, then makes a small river stab of 150 into a ~400 pot. That sizing gives me roughly 27% pot odds (150 to win ≈550). My hand is behind any A‑x, any 6, and most two‑pair/full‑house combos, but it beats missed diamond draws and pure bluffs. Given his passive line into the river, his range is likely capped (weak A’s, medium pairs, missed draws) and the small sizing is consistent with thin value or a bluff. Raising would be a poor overbluff with little fold equity; folding surrenders too much equity for a tiny price. Calling 150 is the least‑worst, +EV play — cheap showdown to catch bluffs and realize my equity.

I am on the button with Qd 8c. This is a marginal hand, but in a three-handed game, it's often worth a raise to try and take the blinds or play in position. Minni has a short stack (43.75 BBs), which makes them more likely to either fold or shove. Elon has a healthy stack. Given the current situation and my goal to maximize my stack, a standard raise of 3x the big blind is appropriate here to exert pressure, especially since Minni has been playing relatively conservatively with their stack lately.

A3o is a weak offsuit ace with poor post-flop playability, especially out of position in the small blind vs a button raise. Flash likely has a wide raising range from the button, but my hand is dominated by better aces and has little potential. Calling 250 OOP risks tough spots later with bad equity. Minni's short stack might shove, complicating things. Folding preserves my deep stack (19k+) for better opportunities late in the 150-hand session.

I have 2♥6♦ in the big blind facing a button open to 300. The pot is 450 and it costs me 200 to call, so I need roughly 30.8% equity to break even. 2‑6 offsuit has very poor equity and playability against a button opening range, and I’ll be out of position postflop. A tiny 3‑bet bluff is possible but low EV here — I’ll get called or 4‑bet by hands that dominate me and risk a difficult spot with little equity. With ~44bb effective and many better spots to invest in, fold and conserve chips.

3h4c is a weak offsuit hand with minimal playability. On the button, we have position, but calling 100 into a 150 pot for speculative value risks difficult post-flop spots against likely calling ranges from blinds. Raising to steal is possible but suboptimal with this holding against aggressive opponents like Minni and Flash who defend frequently based on history. Folding preserves our deep stack (19125) for premium hands in this late stage (hand 133/150) where ICM-like considerations favor tight play to maximize EV.

I’m in the small blind with QcJs (QJ offsuit), ~43 BB effective (stack 4,325) and it’s heads-up vs the big blind (Flash). This is a standard spot to open rather than limp or flat-call — QJ is too strong to play passively out of position. I’ll raise to 300 (3 BB) to take the initiative, gain fold equity against the BB’s wide defending range, and define the pot size. 300 is a clean, table-standard size that avoids giving good odds to speculative hands and still keeps postflop play manageable with 43 BB effective. Postflop plan: if called I’ll c-bet favorable, dry flops and pot-control or check stronger/connected boards; fold to large, polarized 3-bets, and consider calling small 3-bets to play postflop for position/SPR. Overall this is a balanced, +EV open from the SB.

I have Ace-Four suited in the big blind. Minni, in the small blind, has raised to 300. Minni's stack is quite short (about 40bb total). While A4s is a strong hand to defend or even 3-bet with, Minni's short stack and aggressive opening range from the small blind suggest I should at least see a flop. A 3-bet might force a fold from her worse hands or commit her with hands that dominate me. Given the pot odds (200 to call into a 400 pot), calling is a very standard play to see a flop with a hand that has good post-flop potential and nut-flush possibilities.

Flop: 8♥ Q♦ 5♥. I hold Q♣J♠ — top pair with a decent kicker, but out of position versus the big blind who called my preflop raise. Flash’s defending range is fairly wide (Qx, broadways, suited hands, small pairs), so my hand is ahead of a lot of his range but vulnerable to sets/two-pair and I need to charge two-heart/backdoor draws. Standard play is to c-bet for value and protection: a half-pot bet forces equity-drawing hands to pay and extracts value from worse Qx and some Jx/broadways. I’ll bet 300 (≈½ pot). If he folds I collect; if he calls I’ll pot-control on most blank turns and re-evaluate on hearts/overcards; if he check-raises big I’ll assign a very strong, polarized range and fold or proceed only with a clear plan based on sizing.

I have Ace-high with a backdoor nut flush draw on a Queen-high board. Minni's continuation bet is half the size of the pot. Given the wide range Minni typically plays from the small blind and my 4-to-1 pot odds, calling one bet to see if the turn brings an Ace or a diamond draw is a standard defensive play.

I have top pair (Q♣J♠) out of position on a two-heart flop. Flash called my preflop open and my flop c-bet, so his range is wide (Qx, Jx, heart draws, small pairs, some floats). The 7♣ is a relatively blank turn that doesn’t complete obvious draws, so this is a good card to charge hearts and extract value from worse Qx/Jx while still keeping the pot controllable. I’ll bet ~½ pot (600 into 1200): big enough to make draws pay and get value from worse pairs, but small enough to fold to a strong polarized check-raise without committing my stack. If he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the river and often pot-control; if he check-raises large I’ll give him a polarized strong range and fold.

On the turn, the board is 8h-Qd-5h-7c. I have Ace-high (Ad4d) with one diamond. Minni, who started the hand with a relatively short stack, has fired a second barrel of 600 into an 1800 pot. My Ace-high has very little showdown value now, and the 7c doesn't help my hand. While the pot odds are 4-to-1, my outs are limited to Aces or perhaps hitting a lucky diamond on the river for a small bluff, but Minni's commitment to the pot suggests she has a piece of this board (likely a Queen or a draw she is semi-bluffing). Continuing here with just Ace-high and no real draw is minus-EV against a short-stacked opponent who is likely to jam or call any river bet. It is best to fold and wait for a better spot.

I’m on the button with J♠7♥ and about 49–50bbs effective. Pot is 150, call is 100. J7o is marginal but on the button it’s profitable to open a fairly wide range — I get position and can apply pressure to the blinds. Limping surrenders initiative and gives the big blind good odds to play; a small raise to the table standard (300) preserves fold equity, matches my prior opening sizing, and keeps postflop playability in position. Plan: open to 300; fold to a large 3-bet (J7o doesn’t play well vs a polarized 3-bet), but if called I’ll use positional advantage to c-bet or probe depending on the flop texture. Raising maximizes EV here.

I am in the Small Blind with 6s 5d. The Button (Minni) has opened to 300. 65 offsuit is too weak to defend out of position against an open raise, particularly with a short-ish stack on the button who could be pushing a wider range but still likely dominates me, and a large stack in the Big Blind yet to act. Folding is the standard and safest play here.

Preflop in BB facing a button raise to 300 after SB folds. Pot 450, calling 200 more (total 300 from me). A♥3♠ is a marginal defending hand OOP vs button range. Button ranges are wide (~40-50%), and A3o has ~38-40% equity heads-up vs that, plus live ace blocker. Pot odds are excellent (2.25:1), so calling realizes equity cheaply. Post-flop, I can play cautiously OOP with ace-high or fold to c-bets when missing. 3-betting is possible as a bluff with ace blocker but sizing to ~900-1200 preferred over min-raise; however, calling is higher EV here given deep stacks and playability mix. Folding too tight given odds and range advantage.

Heads-up on the flop as OOP in BB with A♥3♠ on Tc 2h 9c board. Hand is ace-high with no pair, no straight or flush draw. Board has two clubs (Tc 9c), presenting flush draws and possible straight draws (e.g., J8, QJ, T8), which favor villain's wide button raising range over my calling range. Equity is poor (~25-35% vs. their range), and no strong blockers beyond live ace. Preflop call was for pot odds and playability, but now check to realize equity cheaply or fold to aggression. Betting would be a bluff, but sizing is tricky OOP (pot 650), and villain likely check-calls light or bets strong hands/draws. Checking preserves stack, induces bluffs/checks from weaker hands, and avoids bloating pot OOP with marginal hand. Plan: check, fold to most bets unless tiny.

Flop is Tc-2h-9c, I have J♠7♥ on the button and Elon (BB) checked. My hand is a pure bluff/semibluff: one overcard to the Ten and a single-card gutshot to an 8 (few outs), no club. The BB’s calling range vs a BTN open is wide (broadways, small/medium pairs, suited connectors, some clubs), so a small-to-medium continuation bet will pick off many unmade hands and deny correct odds to club-draws while keeping the pot manageable if I’m called. I’ll size to about 35–40% of the pot to maximize fold equity without overcommitting (pot ~650 → bet 250). Plan: fold to a large check-raise; if called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and barrel blanks or check behind when appropriate.

Facing a 250 bet into 650 pot (pot odds ~2.6:1) OOP with Ah3s on Tc2h9c board. Hand is pure ace-high bluff-catcher with no pair, no straight/flush draw, and blocked only by live Ah (weakens some Ax value). Board favors villain's wide button range (many flush draws like JcXc, 8cXc; gutshots T8/J8; pairs 99/T9/22). My equity ~25-30% vs. their polarized betting range (strong value like sets/T9/99, draws, bluffs). Calling realizes thin equity but bloats pot OOP with reverse position disadvantage on turn; multiway risk if Flash wakes up unlikely. Raising as bluff overplays hand without fold equity given Minni's history of calling stations (e.g., hands 4,8,13). Preflop call was thin for odds/dead money; folding flop preserves stack for better spots in deep 18k stack game. Discipline key vs. aggressive button.

I am on the button with Q3 offsuit. While it's a 3-handed game, this hand is too weak to open raise, especially with a short-stacked aggressive player in the big blind (Minni) who might shove and a deep-stacked player in the small blind (Elon) who has been playing quite actively. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Preflop in small blind after button fold. Hand 6c8d offsuit is marginal but has straight potential and playability. Pot 150, call 50 more for 3:1 odds, excellent. Folding SB here is too tight; opponents defend wide in BB. No raise needed as it's not a stealing spot with this hand. Plan to play postflop based on board texture. Minni's stack 5225 allows for ICM considerations but deep enough for this play.

I’m in the big blind with Tc8c facing a limp from Elon (SB). Heads-up limps usually represent a wide, often passive range — Tc8c is a playable, suited connector with good postflop potential and blocker value. I want to take the initiative, isolate the limp, and avoid giving a free flop, so I’ll raise to a standard size to both get value from worse hands and fold out some total air. Size chosen (300 total) keeps the pot manageable with deep stacks but applies pressure; plan is to C-bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously if I face strong resistance (fold to a sizable 3-bet, continue vs single call).

Preflop in SB with 6c8d offsuit after limping. Minni's raise to 300 from BB after my limp indicates strength or a wide defense/steal. While pot odds are decent (calling 200 into 400 for ~3:1), 68o is a marginal speculative hand with poor postflop playability out of position heads-up. OOP, it plays poorly against a raiser's range, which likely includes strong hands, pairs, and broadways. No strong reason to raise as a bluff given deep stacks and my image. Folding preserves stack for better spots late in the session (hand 135/150), avoiding difficult OOP situations with reverse implied odds if I hit a weak draw or pair.

Preflop on the button with KTo offsuit, facing a small bet of 100 into a 150 pot (likely a limp or completion from SB and check from BB). Excellent position with deep stacks. KTo is a strong button raising hand with good high-card strength, playability, and blocker value. Opponents have wide defending ranges from blinds, and Minni in SB has a shorter stack (~54BB). Standard play is to raise for value, initiative, and fold equity. Raising to 4BB (400 total) is a good size to build the pot while maintaining balance. Will fold weak hands postflop if missed, but can continue with position.

Holding Q♥5♠ in the small blind facing a button open to 400 (pot 550). To call I must put in 350 which gives ~38.9% pot odds, but Q5o is a weak, frequently dominated hand against a BTN opening range and performs poorly multiway — Flash (BB) often defends/calls here, which would further reduce my equity and leave me out of position vs both players. A 3-bet bluff has poor blocker value and unnecessarily bloats the pot with a marginal holding; I want to preserve my ~53.8BB stack for better spots. Fold and wait for a cleaner opportunity.

I am in the Big Blind with pocket eights, which is a very strong hand in a three-handed game. Elon has raised to 400 from the button, which is a wide opening position. While his sizing is slightly larger than the typical 3x, 88 is a premium hand that benefits from a 3-bet for both value and protection against overcards. By raising to 1200, I take the initiative out of position and can often win the pot pre-flop or set up a favorable post-flop scenario.

Preflop on the button with KTo offsuit facing a 3-bet to 1200 (3x) from the aggressive BB Flash after my open to 400. Flash has shown a history of frequent 3-bets, suggesting a polarized range including value (QQ+, AK) and bluffs (suited aces, connectors). KTo blocks kings, has decent high-card strength, and plays well postflop in position with deep stacks (~180BB effective). Equity against a typical BB 3-bet range is around 38-42%, and pot odds are favorable (calling 800 into 1650 for ~3:1 direct, plus implied). Calling balances my range, allows controlling pot size OOP for opponent, and exploits position to peel floats or value bet hits. Folding is too tight given history and blockers; 4-betting risks bloating pot OOP with marginal hand lacking premium blockers. Standard call to realize equity.

On a monotone flop of 3s-6s-7s, my hand (pocket eights with the 8 of spades) is exceptionally strong. I have an overpair to the board, an open-ended straight draw, and an 8-high flush draw. Since I was the pre-flop 3-better, this board texture generally favors my range, which contains more high spades (As, Ks, Qs) than the button's calling range. I will lead with a continuation bet of approximately 1/3 the pot to extract value from lower pairs, straight draws (like 45 or 58 without spades), and lone spade hands (like Ax or Kx with a spade), while also protecting my hand against overcards.

Flop board 3s6s7s is extremely draw-heavy: nut flush draws, open-enders (45s), wheel draws, pairs with backdoors. KToo has zero made hand, no flush draw, no straight draw (gutshot unlikely), just K high with T backdoor. Preflop calling 3-bet was fine for equity/position, but this flop misses completely. Flash's BB 3-bet range (polarized: QQ+/AK value, suited Ax/connectors bluffs) + donk-lead (small 850 into 3300 ~1/4 pot) reps strong: flush draws (AsXs, Ks9s etc), sets/straights unlikely but pairs+draws (67s, 78s), or monsters. My equity <25% vs value/draw range (blocks some KK but not spades), calling 850 gives ~4:1 direct but implied poor (turn flush likely, OOP he checks weak/I check back). Raising king high bluff lacks blockers/fold equity vs his stubborn lines (history shows calls big pots). Position good but stack preservation key in long session; fold marginal missed flop to live for better spots with history of aggression elsewhere.

I'm on the button with 3h9h (suited, but a weak hand). Pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see the flop. Versus the SB (Flash) and BB (Elon) both of whom defend and raise fairly often, a min-raise here will likely get called or 3‑bet; I don't want to face a sizable 3‑bet out of position or inflate the pot with a marginal hand. I have position postflop, which is more valuable for extracting equity with a suited hand like 93s. Plan: call cheaply to see a flop in position, c‑bet only on favorable runouts (heart/connected/drawy boards) or check and control the pot on dry boards; fold to a large preflop 3‑bet. Therefore I will call the 100 to see the flop.

I am holding pocket deuces in the small blind. The button (Minni) has limped in. With a speculative hand like a small pocket pair, the primary goal is to hit a set on the flop. I only need to call 50 more chips to see the flop, which offers excellent pot odds. I will call to try and set mine, keeping the pot small for now.

Preflop in the big blind with 5d6c offsuit connectors. Both opponents have limped, creating a multiway pot of 300. This hand has speculative value for straights and flushes/draws postflop, especially multiway with position out of position but deep stacks (except Minni shorter at 5k). Raising risks isolation with a marginal hand that plays poorly heads-up OOP, while checking keeps the pot small and realizes equity on favorable flops. Standard play is to check and see the flop.

On this board of Qs-Jd-5c, my pocket deuces are very weak. With two overcards to my pair (Queen and Jack) and two opponents, it is highly likely one of them has hit a piece of this board or has a better pocket pair. However, as the action is currently checked to me and the pot is small, there is no need to fold yet. I will check and see how the action develops. If there is a bet, I will likely fold as I have very little equity and no real draw.

Middle pair with a weak 6 kicker on a Qs Jd 5c flop in a multiway pot OOP. Board is coordinated with straight draws (e.g., KT, T9, 87) and a club flush draw possible. Limpers' ranges are wide, including overcards, draws, and better pairs. Flash's check suggests weakness. Betting risks raising out equity or getting raised by better; checking controls pot size, realizes equity multiway on turns that improve (e.g., 4x, 7x for straight), and allows seeing Minni's reaction on button. Preflop plan was to check/call flops profitably, and this fits. Positionally disadvantaged, so pot control prioritizes stack preservation over thin value.

Flop is Qs Jd 5c and I hold 9h3h — no pair, only a very remote backdoor heart/straight potential. Both opponents checked; this board heavily hits typical preflop calling ranges (Qx/Jx/Ax/10x) so my fold equity is low and a c-bet will often be called by better hands. My preflop plan was to call and only c-bet on favorable, drawy runouts (hearts or coordinated boards). This is a dry, high-card flop that favors villains, and the pot is still small while stacks are deep. I want to control the pot and see a free turn where I can continue only if I pick up real equity (a heart, a 9/3, or a very favorable blank). Therefore I check. If I face small turn aggression I’ll fold; if a heart or a low connecting card comes I’ll consider a semi-bluff or a delayed c-bet.

The turn card is a 6h, which doesn't change much for my pocket deuces. Since everyone checked the flop, it's possible no one has a strong hand, but with two overcards (Queen and Jack) on the board, my deuces have very little showdown value. I will check again to see the river for free. If anyone bets, I will fold, as I am not looking to invest chips in this hand without significant improvement.

On the turn, the 6h pairs my 6c, giving me two pair (6s and 5s) with the board Qs Jd 5c 6h. This is a strong hand in a multiway pot of 300, disguisable as a bluff or draw. No completed straights are obvious (e.g., no 78 or T7 showed), but club flush draw exists, and hearts are open. Flash's check suggests weakness or trap, but given history of passive play, likely weak. Minni (short stack 5275) limped preflop with wide button range including overcards, pairs, draws. Betting protects against draws, gets value from worse pairs/draws (e.g., Qx, Jx, Ax, sets unlikely), and denies equity. Half-pot bet (150) is small but builds pot without scaring off callers; larger risks fold-out worse hands. Pot control not needed with strength OOP. Prior flop plan realized equity perfectly.

I checked the flop with 9h3h to control the pot. The turn 6h gives me no real improvement: flush is impossible (only one heart on board plus my two = at most 4 hearts by river), and my only realistic outs are pairing the 9 or 3 (3 + 3 = 6 outs). That’s roughly 6/46 ≈ 13% equity to improve. Elon’s small turn bet of 150 into a 450 pot means a call would have to get ≈150/(450+150+150)=150/750 = 20% equity to be profitable. My estimated equity (~13%) is well below that, and even if I hit I’m often still behind hands like Qx/Jx or other pairs. Raising would be a thin, high-variance bluff out of position with two players still involved. Fold and preserve the stack for a better spot.

I am holding pocket deuces on a board with two overcards (Queen and Jack). After checking both the flop and the turn, the big blind (Elon) has led out with a small bet of 150 into a 300 pot. While the price to call is cheap, my hand has almost no equity and very little showdown value against his range. I only have two outs to improve to a set on the river, and I am likely already crushed by any pair or high card. Given the texture of the board and the fact that I am out of position, I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I am on the button with Qd 4d. In a three-handed game, Q4 suited is a standard opening hand from the button to put pressure on the blinds and take advantage of position. My stack is healthy, and while Minni is somewhat short-stacked, I have enough leverage to fold if they shove over my open. I will raise to 3x the big blind.

Pocket 7s is a strong premium hand preflop, roughly 82% equity against a typical button opening range. Flash's raise from the button to 300 (3x BB) is standard but likely wide, including suited connectors, broadway, Ax, pairs, and bluffs/steals given position. As SB, calling puts me OOP with poor postflop position, so 3-betting is optimal to isolate (especially vs Minni's 51BB effective stack), deny equity to worse hands, build a pot when ahead, and take initiative. Sizing to 900 (3x the raise) is standard, balancing value and pressure. Minni may fold, call, or jam her ~52BB stack, but 77 crushes her likely calling/shoving range. Folding is too tight; set-mining OOP is suboptimal with deep stacks.

Flash opened to 300 (button) and Elon 3-bet to 900 from the SB. I’m in the big blind with A♦6♥ (offsuit). Pot is 1300 and it costs me 800 to continue. A6o is a marginal hand here: it’s often dominated by a SB 3-bet range and performs poorly out-of-position, especially if the original raiser (Button) still acts and comes along — multiway pots are bad for a one-pair/ace‑kicker type holding. Calling requires ~38% equity to break even preflop; A6o vs a reasonable 3-bet range usually has less, and there’s little implied odds OOP against two big stacks. The min‑raise to 1500 would be a weak, thin 4‑bet and shove is far too committal for a dominated hand. With ~51 BB I’ll conserve most of my stack for better spots and fold this marginal hand to the 3‑bet.

I opened from the button with Qd 4d and Elon has 3-bet from the small blind. While Q4s is a reasonable opening hand, it is quite weak against a 3-bet from a competent opponent. Given that I have already invested 300 and need to call another 600 into a pot of 1300, the pot odds are okay, but the playability of Q4s post-flop out of position against a 3-bettor is poor. Elon has shown aggression and solid play in previous hands. Folding is the disciplined move here to protect my stack and wait for a better spot, as I don't want to get involved in a bloated pot with a marginal hand.

Preflop on the button with 4s2c, a complete trash hand with no high card value, poor connectivity, and offsuit. No position advantage justifies entering the pot voluntarily against the blinds. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots, especially near the end of 150 hands where discipline maximizes EV.

2♦6♦ is a very weak hand and out of position against the big blind. Folding forfeits only the 50 small blind, but raising to 200 would be spewy with virtually no fold equity vs a decent BB defender and would put me in many difficult spots postflop. Calling (completing to 100 total) is the cheapest way to realize equity and see a flop with backdoor flush/straight potential while preserving my 51bb stack. Postflop plan: proceed only with a clear piece (pair, two pair, trips) or strong draws; otherwise check-fold to significant aggression. Therefore I will call the 50 to complete to 100 total.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Jack offsuit, which is a very strong hand in a three-handed game. Minni, in the Small Blind, just completed the blind. Given her relatively short stack of approximately 50 big blinds (5075 chips), this is a good opportunity to put pressure on her or build a pot with a superior range. I will raise to 300 to take control of the hand and potentially win the pot immediately or see a flop with the initiative.

2♦6♦ is a very weak hand and out of position, so I won’t 3‑bet or shove — that would be spewy. However the cost to see a flop is small: the pot is 400 and I need to put in 200 more, so I’m getting ~3:1 pot odds (~25% equity required). Flash opens to 300 here a lot, so his range can be fairly wide; 2♦6♦ has some backdoor diamond and straight potential and can realize equity cheaply. I’ll call to see a flop and proceed only with a real piece (pair/two pair/trips) or strong draws (nut-ish flush/open-ender); otherwise I’ll fold to significant aggression OOP.

I flopped a clear piece — middle pair (6s) with 2♦6♦ on 6♣-5♥-Q♦. I’m out of position against Flash, the preflop raiser, whose continuation range will include a lot of Qx and many hands that will call a bet (pocket pairs, broadways, suited A/Kx). Betting here often gets called by better hands and commits me OOP with a mediocre kicker; checking lets me control the pot and induce bluffs. Plan: check to keep the pot manageable, call small-to-moderate c-bets to realize equity and extract value from worse pairs/draws, and fold to large raises unless I improve. If Flash checks back I’ll take a free turn card and re-evaluate. Therefore I will check.

On a flop of 6c-5h-Qd, I have Ace-high with one overcard to the Queen. As the pre-flop aggressor, I will put out a standard continuation bet of slightly more than half the pot to maintain my initiative and fold out hands that missed the flop. Minni's check-call pre-flop suggests a wide but likely marginal range. If she has a Queen or a pocket pair, she will likely call, but a bet here can win the pot immediately against her folding range.

I have middle pair (6s) with a weak kicker and I’m out of position. Flash’s 325 into a 925 pot is a small-to-moderate continuation bet — I’m getting roughly 3:1 pot odds (~25–30% equity required) and my pair has reasonable equity versus a wide c-bet range that contains lots of Qx, some overcards, and bluffs. Raising OOP with a single pair and a poor kicker is too thin: it bloats the pot and puts me in many difficult spots against better Qx/overpairs. Calling controls the pot, allows me to realize equity, and keeps the option to fold to significant turn aggression unless I improve. Plan: call 325 now; if I hit two-pair/trips or a strong draw on the turn I’ll extract, but fold to large turn/river barrels when I haven’t improved.

The turn gave me a concrete piece plus a huge draw: I have middle pair (6s) and now a four‑card diamond draw (2♦6♦ + Q♦4♦ on board) plus a gutshot to a 3 and two remaining 6s — roughly ~14 outs total, so strong equity to the river. Flash checked the turn after betting the flop, which looks like pot‑control with Qx/medium pairs or missed overcards; this is a good spot to extract value and charge his draws/overcards rather than just check and give him a free river. I’ll make a value/pressure stab about half the pot to get called by worse pairs, Qx, A/K type hands and to charge diamond/straight draws, while keeping the pot controllable if he shows aggression. If he check‑raises big I’ll re‑evaluate (likely fold to very large raises); if he calls I plan to re‑assess on the river and only commit further with improvement or clear odds.

On the turn card 4d, the board is 6c-5h-Qd-4d. Minni has led out with a bet of 600 into a pot of 1250 after check-calling the flop. This 'donk' bet often represents a piece of the board that just improved (like a straight draw getting there or a two-pair) or a marginal Queen trying to protect its equity. With Ace-high and no real draw other than a back-door straight, I am likely behind her range. However, Minni's stack is quite short (3950 remaining), and calling here commits a significant portion of my stack if I intend to see a river. Given that she led into the pre-flop aggressor on a board that connects reasonably well with a blind-completion range (65, 54, 78, etc.), I will fold and wait for a better spot, as my current hand has very little showdown value or potential to improve.

Three-handed on the button with Q♠5♠. In a 3‑way game the button should be opening a wide range — Q5s is a playable, suited queen with good post‑flop playability and positional advantage. The table’s standard open sizing has been ~300, and with 58bb effective I want a small, standard open to take the initiative and pressure the blinds while keeping room to maneuver post‑flop. Limping surrenders initiative and invites cheap multiway pots against aggressive players, folding is too tight. If I face a large, committed 3‑bet I’ll concede this hand, but versus a single call I’ll play in position and c‑bet when appropriate. Raise to 300 (standard 3bb open).

I am in the Small Blind with J9o. The button (Minni) has raised to 3x (300). Minni's stack is relatively short (55bb) compared to the start of the game, and they have been quite active. J9o is a marginal hand that typically doesn't play well out of position against a raise. While I could defend, folding is the most disciplined play to avoid being out of position in a three-way pot or heads-up against an aggressive opponent with a mediocre hand.

45 suited connectors are a solid speculative hand in the big blind facing a button raise, especially with good implied odds given Minni's shorter stack of 5500 and my deep stack. Minni raises frequently from the button as seen in prior hands, suggesting a wide range including many hands I dominate or can outdraw. Position is OOP postflop, but playability is strong with flush and straight potential. Pot odds are favorable: calling 200 more (total 300 into growing pot). Folding is too tight, 3-betting risks bloating pot OOP with a marginal hand. Calling to realize equity and control pot size.

Flop Ac Tc 3d gives me a powerful combo draw: open-ended straight draw (8 outs to 2 or 6) plus flush draw (9 diamond outs), roughly 35-40% equity against most of Minni's button opening range, which is wide based on history (frequent raises with marginal hands). OOP as BB first to act, but deep stacks and Minni's shorter effective stack (5500). Betting semi-bluffs to apply pressure, deny equity to weaker pairs/draws (e.g., gutshots, lower flush draws), build the pot when ahead, and realize fold equity given Minni's fold to aggression in similar spots. Check risks giving free card to overcards or checks back weak hands; check-raise possible but donk bet sizes pot well. 2/3 pot bet (450) is balanced size for value/protection.

I opened wide on the button with Q♠5♠ which is fine preflop, but this flop A♣ T♣ 3♦ is very unfavorable for my hand. Elon called preflop and now leads with 450 into a 1,100 pot — calling 450 would require ~29% equity (450/(1100+450) ≈ 29%). I only have 6 clean outs (three Qs and three 5s) → ~24% to pair by the river, and many of those pairs still lose to Ax, Tx or pocket pairs. I have no club or meaningful backdoor draws, so my true equity vs his c-bet range is well below the required threshold. Raising as a bluff to 900 is too thin — he can comfortably call with Ax, clubs and many pairs. Preserve stack and avoid a marginal call/raise with very little fold equity or equity to improve. Fold.

I am on the button with A8o and 160.25 big blinds. A8o is a standard opening hand from the button in a 3-handed game. My stack is healthy, and Minni is relatively short-stacked with only 54 big blinds. Opening to 300 (3x the big blind) is a standard play to put pressure on the blinds and take control of the pot early.

K9h is a suited king, strong enough to defend from the small blind against a button raise due to excellent implied odds, flush and straight potential, and domination of worse kings. Pot odds are favorable: calling 250 into 450 (pot will be larger after BB acts) provides about 2:1 direct odds with much more implied. Flash's button raise range is wide (likely 40%+ hands based on history of frequent raises from late position). Out of position but stacks are deep, and Minni in BB is relatively short at 54BB, may act aggressively or fold. Folding is too tight; 3-betting risks playing OOP with marginal hand vs wide range. Prefer calling to realize equity multiway or heads-up, control pot OOP, and exploit Flash's aggression postflop.

Hand is T♦2♠ — a very weak, poorly-connected, often-dominated hand. Facing a button open to 300 and a small‑blind call creates a multiway pot; calling 200 more gives me ~22% break‑even equity (200 to win ~900) but T2o’s realized equity multiway vs these ranges is likely below that. 3‑betting as a bluff with this hand is unattractive (poor blockers, likely to get called by better hands and put in difficult post‑flop spots). With a 5,400 stack I should conserve chips for better spots rather than investing another 200 into a marginal, multiway pot. Fold and save the remainder of my stack.

On this flop of 5d Ks Js, I have top pair with Kh (second kicker 9h), which is vulnerable on a highly coordinated board with straight draws (QT, T10), flush draws (spades), and possible two pairs or sets in Flash's button raising range. Heads-up OOP after calling preflop with suited king, pot control is key. Flash's wide button range includes bluffs, draws, and weaker pairs that I beat, plus overpairs or better. Betting risks getting raised off equity or building pot with marginal strength; checking induces bluffs, allows cheap showdowns, and realizes equity against his aggression (evident from history of frequent continuation bets). If he checks behind, great; small bet callable; large bet foldable to preserve stack. Deep stacks support this passive line to maximize EV long-term.

On a flop of Ks Js 5d, my Ace-high (Ad 8s) has some showdown value but doesn't interact well with this board. This texture is generally better for the caller's range (SB), as it contains many hands like Kx, Jx, and straight draws (QT, T9). While a continuation bet is often standard, checking back allows me to control the pot and see a turn card for free, keeping the pot small with a hand that cannot withstand much pressure if check-raised.

On the turn with board 5d Ks Js Jh, I hold top pair (kings) with a weak 9 kicker in a heads-up pot OOP against Flash's wide button range. The board is highly coordinated pre-turn (spade flush draw, open-enders like QT, T9), and the Jh pairs the Js, improving some draws (e.g., spade flush draw now has backdoor heart outs potentially) and hitting possible Jx hands, two-pairs, or trips. Flash's flop check-behind after my check suggests possible weakness (missed flop, air/draw slowing down) or trapping with strong hands, but his aggressive history supports checking to induce bluffs/value thin bets while controlling pot size. Betting risks raising off equity against better (KQ/KA/KD, sets, JJ, KJ) or building unnecessary pot OOP where I have showdown value against worse (weaker Kx, Ax high, bluffs). Deep effective stacks (~25BB pot) favor passive line: check to realize equity cheaply, pot control, and exploit Flash's likely aggression on river. Free river card possible if he checks again. Marginal strength doesn't warrant bluffing turn OOP.

On a board of 5d-Ks-Js-Jh, the Jack has paired. I have Ace-high (Ad 8s). My opponent (Elon) has checked twice (flop and turn). While the Jack pairing makes it less likely he has one, my Ace-high still has significant showdown value in a small pot. Checking back here allows me to realize that showdown value for free or evaluate on the river if he leads out. Betting doesn't accomplish much as better hands (Kx, pairs) likely won't fold, and I'm ahead of most of his air/draws anyway.

On the river, the board is 5d Ks Js Jh Kd, and I hold Kh9h, giving me trip kings with a 9 kicker—a monster hand that beats any two-pair, most straights (e.g., QT would have played differently), flushes (unlikely given Flash's passive check-check-check line), and only loses to full houses like JJ (kings full of jacks) or quads (extremely unlikely). Flash's button raise preflop is wide, but his triple check (flop, turn, river so far OOP for him? No, I'm OOP) suggests weakness: missed draws, weaker pairs (KJ, AJ, QJ), or air/bluffs. Deep stacks (18k effective vs 15k), pot 700. OOP first to act, but this hand warrants a value bet to extract from worse (top pair like KJ/AJ with weak kicker, Jx pairs, overcards). He checked behind flop/turn, likely to pay off thin value or call with showdown value. Sizing: 50-60% pot (~350-450) balances value/targeting his range without scaring off too much; 400 extracts max EV given history of calling stations in spots. Folding/checking misses value; overbet risks fold equity too high against passive line.

On a board of 5d-Ks-Js-Jh-Kd, the board is double-paired with Kings and Jacks. My best five-card hand is K-K-J-J-A (using the Ace from my hole cards). This beats any hand that doesn't contain a King, a Jack, or an Ace, as those hands would use the board's 5 as a kicker. Since I have the highest possible kicker (the Ace), I only lose to a full house (any King or Jack). Elon checked both the flop and the turn, which makes a slow-played King or Jack less likely, though not impossible. He is leading into me on the river for 400 into a 700 pot. Given that I win against all bluffs and pocket pairs (like 66-QQ) and split the pot with any other Ace (like AQ, AT), I am getting excellent pot odds (3.75 to 1) to call. Raising is unnecessary because he likely won't call a raise with a hand worse than an Ace and will certainly call or re-raise with a full house.

7s4d is a complete trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high cards. On the button with position, but facing a to-call of 100 into 150 pot (pot odds ~1.5:1), the hand has zero equity against any reasonable range. Minni in SB has a small stack of 5350, but playing this hand risks chips unnecessarily late in the tournament (hand 142/150) when survival and stack maximization are key. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

Heads-up in the small blind with J♦6♦ — a marginal but playable suited hand. The pot is 150 and it costs me 50 to complete (to 100 total), so I only need ~25% equity to make a call profitable. Flash (BB) defends widely and rarely folds to steals, so a raise to 200 likely won’t gain much fold equity and can just inflate the pot or induce a 3-bet. With ~53 BB effective I prefer to see a flop in position (I’m on the button postflop) and realize equity/positional advantage. Call the 50 (total committed = 100).

I am in the Big Blind with T9o. Minni, who is relatively short-stacked (53 BBs), has completed from the Small Blind. This is a very weak play that usually indicates a wide range of middling or speculative hands. T9o is a decent hand with good playability in a limped pot. I have the option to check and see a free flop or raise to put pressure on Minni. Given the current game state and the fact that there are only a few hands left, checking is a low-variance play that allows me to realize my equity and evaluate the board. Since I am in position for the rest of the hand (as it is a heads-up pot after the button folded), I will check and take the free flop.

I’ve flopped trips: J♦6♦ on 8♦ 6♠ 6♣. This is a very strong hand but the paired board makes full houses/quads possible (pocket 8s or the remaining 6). Flash checked the flop and defends wide preflop, so his range contains many one-pair hands (8x, weaker pocket pairs, Kx/Qx) that will pay to be bet against. I want to extract value and deny free cards that could pair the 8 or 6, so I’ll size a value/protection bet to ~3/4 pot to get called by worse hands while charging the few hands that can improve. Plan: bet 150 now; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and continue extracting. If he check-raises big, I’ll assume a very strong hand and re-evaluate based on sizing and villain tendencies (call smaller raises, be cautious to an overbet jam).

In this limped pot, Minni has led out for 150 into 200 on a board of 8d-6s-6c. My hand, T9o, has two overcards and a backdoor straight draw. Given the excellent pot odds (calling 150 to win a total pot of 500) and my position, a call is standard here. I want to see how the turn develops. If Minni is betting with air or a weak 8, I have equity to improve or can potentially outplay her on later streets in position.
