Raising here doesn't accomplish much as I'd likely only be called by better hands (trips or an 8) and fold out the bluffs I beat.

Turn puts a Jack on the board and pairs my Jack — I now have a very strong full house (three sixes with a pair of Jacks: 666JJ). Very few hands beat me here; the main realistic concern is pocket 88 (which makes eights full of sixes). Pocket 66/quads and trip JJJ aren’t possible given I hold a 6 and a J. Flash called my flop raise and defends wide, so his range includes pocket pairs (including 88) and other medium-strength hands that will happily call a sizable value bet. I want to extract value from 88 and similar hands and build the pot while still keeping room to respond if he check-raises. Pot is ~500 — I’ll bet big enough to get called by second-best hands but not so huge it scares them off. Betting 1500 accomplishes that: it charges 88 and most two-pair/overpair hands, builds a pot I can comfortably commit to, and still lets me fold/re-evaluate only to an extremely polarized overbet. Action: bet 1500.

On a board of 8d-6s-6c-Jc, Minni has led for 1500 into a pot of 500. This massive 3x pot overbet indicates either a very strong hand (like a 6 or a slow-played monster) or a complete air bluff intended to force a fold. My current hand is Ten-high with no significant draws or showdown value. While Minni's line is suspicious, I cannot justify calling such a large bet with absolutely no hand. Folding is the only sensible play here as I am not looking to gamble my stack on a pure guess against a short-stacked opponent who is likely pot-committed if I were to raise.

Button in a 3-handed pot with K♠4♣. With only three players the opening range should be wide and positional advantage is significant — K4o is marginal but worth opening. I want fold equity and to take the initiative, but I don't want to bloat the pot with a speculative hand. A standard ~3bb open is appropriate: big enough to pick up the blinds often, small enough to keep pots manageable if called. Plan postflop: C-bet on favorable, non-Ace/very dry boards; fold to a large 3-bet preflop or big aggression that commits a lot of chips since K4o is easily dominated. Therefore I raise to 300 (total).

In a three-handed game, Ace-Seven offsuit is a relatively strong hand when facing a button raise. Minni's 3x open is standard, and while I am out of position, calling the 250 additional chips is the most balanced play. It allows me to see a flop without bloating the pot or risking a shove from Minni's 50BB stack if I were to 3-bet. I will look to evaluate based on the flop texture.

3h4h is a speculative suited connector with good playability multi-way. Excellent pot odds: calling 200 more into a pot that will be 900 post-call (3.5:1 direct, better implied). Deep stacks allow strong implied odds if I hit straights, flushes, or two-pair. Button raise and SB flat suggest a wide range, and history shows loose multi-way action. Calling is standard BB defense here; raising risks too much with marginal strength against two opponents. Post-flop, I'll have position after blinds if checked to, but proceed cautiously.

On this King-Jack-Trey rainbow board, I have completely missed with my Ace-Seven offsuit. Minni (the pre-flop raiser) and Elon (the big blind) are both still in the hand. This board texture heavily favors Minni's opening range, as it contains many broadway cards. As the small blind, I am out of position. Checking is the standard play here to see how the other players react. If Minni bets, I will likely fold unless the bet is extremely small, as I only have Ace-high and very little back-door equity.

Bottom pair (threes) with a weak four kicker on a king-jack-three rainbow board (one heart matching my suit for backdoor flush draw). Multiway pot with preflop raiser (Minni on button) and a caller (Flash in SB who just checked). Positionally, I act before Minni but have shown strength by calling preflop. Board is dry but king-high favors opponents' wide raising/calling ranges (kings, jacks, overpairs, Ax). Betting risks getting raised by better hands or called only by worse that can improve ahead; pot control is key with marginal made hand and speculative draws. Checking induces bluffs from Minni, keeps pot small (~900), realizes equity on turn (possible 2/5 for straight draws, runner-runner flush), and retains fold equity for future streets. Deep stacks but Minni's 5350 limits aggression. Standard check in BB OOP to button raiser after SB check.

I opened preflop and both opponents just checked to me on a relatively dry K♥ J♠ 3♣ board. I hold K♠4♣ — top pair but a very weak kicker, so my hand has value vs their wide calling ranges (Jx, worse Kx, small pairs, some QJ/Qx) but is vulnerable to better Kx and A‑x. As preflop aggressor this is a textbook spot to c‑bet for value and to deny equity to any backdoor outs. Against two callers I don’t want to overcommit with a bloated size (I can be crushed by better Ks), but I do want to charge hands that will pay off. A medium bet (~50% pot) balances extracting value and keeping my options open post‑turn. I will bet 450 into the 900 pot. Plan: take it down here if both fold; if called, continue cautiously on the turn (check‑call small bets with pot control). If I face a large raise I will strongly lean to folding given my weak kicker and stacks behind.

I have missed the flop completely on a texture that favors the pre-flop raiser (Minni). My Ace-high has very little showdown value and no draws to improve. With one player still to act behind me and Minni's continuation bet on a board containing two broadway cards, folding is the most disciplined play to protect my stack for better opportunities in the final hands.

Facing a 450 bet into 1350 pot after check-check on Js3cKh board. Hold bottom pair threes with four kicker and negligible backdoor heart draw. OOP against button raiser who c-bets wide after two checks, range includes strong value (KJ+, Kx, overpairs) and bluffs but favors better showdown hands or continuation. Pot odds require ~25% equity, but weak kicker vulnerable to domination (A3,23+,34+ better); calling commits to awkward turn OOP with low SPR (Minni ~4900 effective). Folding preserves stack for superior spots in late game stages; speculative preflop call realized minimal equity without improving position or strength. Standard fold marginal bottom pair OOP to agg button.

I have been dealt pocket Jacks (Js Jc) on the button, which is a very strong premium starting hand. The standard play here is to raise to build the pot and protect my equity. A typical opening raise is 3x the big blind, which is 300.

Preflop in small blind facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB) from Flash, who has been aggressive in past hands. My hand Ac3c is suited with the nut flush potential and ace blocker, giving decent playability against a wide button range. Stacks are deep (mine 18k+, opponents similar), pot odds favorable (calling 250 into 450 pot ~36% pot odds). Folding is too tight for SB defense; 3-betting A3s lacks strength and fold equity isn't necessary. Calling to realize equity on flops where I can flop strong draws, pairs, or flush draws, controlling pot OOP postflop if needed. Minni in BB may call or fold wide.

Facing a button raise to 300 with the small blind calling (3‑way pot). Pot is 700 and I must call 200 to play on — calling 200 into a 900 total (700+my 200) requires ~22% equity to break even. My hand 9♥2♥ is one of the weakest playable starting hands: even suited it is very poorly positioned and is frequently dominated in multiway pots. A small 3‑bet (to 500) is unlikely to fold out either opponent and a shove is too spewy with junk. With deep stacks and only marginal pot odds, this is a clear -EV spot. Preserve my 6,150 stack for a better opportunity and fold.

Flop board 9d7s5s is highly draw-heavy with two spades for flush draws and straight draws like 68 (open-ender), A4/T6 (gutshots/wheel possibilities). Preflop, I defended SB wide with suited Ac3c for playability, but flop misses completely: ace-high with no pair, no flush draw (wrong suit), no straight draw (3 doesn't connect well). OOP first to act, betting would be a pure bluff into Flash's wide button raising range which includes many pairs (55-99 good, overpairs), flush draws (spades), straight draws, and better aces. Poor bet sizing EV and fold equity questionable given aggression history. Checking is mandatory for pot control: induces bluffs from Flash's missed draws/overvalue thin bets, allows cheap fold to strong bets, or check-raise if he reps strong. Deep stacks (18k/14k), but no showdown value or equity realization incentive to bloat pot OOP.

I have an overpair (Jacks) on a board of 9-7-5 with a spade draw. This is a very strong but vulnerable hand. Elon checked the flop, which is a standard play for the pre-flop caller. I should bet here to get value from worse hands like pairs of 9s, 7s, or 5s, and to charge any straight or flush draws. A bet of around 60-75% of the pot is standard. The pot is 700, so a bet of 450 is a good size to apply pressure while extracting value.

Facing a 450 bet into 1150 pot on a draw-heavy flop (9d7s5s) where I have complete air (Ac3c: ace-high, no pair, no flush draw as clubs, no straight draw connecting well). Preflop defended SB wide for playability, but flop missed entirely. Flash's button raise + cbet range is strong: overpairs, pocket pairs (88-66 good, sets), spade flush draws, straight draws (68, T6, A4 wheel etc.), which crush my hand. Calling offers poor odds (~3:1 implied but OOP no showdown value, multiway risk if Minni wakes up, turn/river bricks worsen position). Raising bluff lacks fold equity given his aggression history (often calls/raises draws/value) and position; stacks deep but SPR low post-call (~30 SPR, tough to realize bluffs). Folding preserves stack for better spots in final hands of session; equity realization minimal, pot control irrelevant now. Tight fold maximizes EV long-term.

Q3o is a trash hand offsuit with a weak kicker and poor postflop playability. Even on the button in 3-handed play, standard RFI range includes stronger Qx offsuit like Q9o+, but Q3o is easily dominated by Qxo where x>3 and gets outkicked. Limping induces raises and plays OOP poorly. Calling the BB commits chips with marginal equity vs calling ranges. Best to fold and wait for better spots, especially near end of session with deep stacks.

I have Q♦J♠ in the small blind heads-up vs the big blind. This is a hand worth playing for initiative — folding is too tight and calling 50 leaves me out of position with a marginal hand. I’ll raise to 300 (3x the BB) to apply pressure, isolate Flash’s wide BB defending range, and build a manageable pot with decent equity. The sizing is small relative to my ~61BB stack, gives solid fold equity, and keeps postflop SPR reasonable. Plan: c-bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously if he 3-bets (fold to very large 3-bets, call or continue vs smaller ones). Total committed when raising = 300 chips.

I am in the Big Blind holding Ten of Spades and Four of Spades. Elon folded on the Button, and Minni raised to 300 from the Small Blind. I need to call 200 more to see a flop in a pot of 400, giving me 2:1 pot odds. Ten-Four suited is a reasonable hand to defend in position against a Small Blind open, especially with only a few hands remaining in the session. I will call to see a flop.

I raised preflop to take the initiative and my range includes a lot of broadway hands and some Ax/Kx. Flop A♣ 7♠ K♥ is relatively dry but it hits calling ranges (some A/K and pairs) — still many hands in Flash’s range are just air or weak broadways that will fold to pressure. I hold Q♦J♠: no pair but I have a Broadway draw (any Ten completes 10-J-Q-K-A — ~16.5% to improve by river). Betting here for half the pot (300 into 600) accomplishes two things: it folds enough of the weaker parts of his range and charges hands with a single overcard or small pair, while keeping the pot manageable if I get called. Plan: if he folds I take the pot; if he calls I proceed very cautiously — check the turn unless I hit the Ten (or get a very favorable card), and fold to significant turn check-raise or large turn/river aggression since I’m currently behind a lot of his calling range.

On a board of Ace-King-Seven, my Ten-Four of spades has very little equity. Minni raised pre-flop and is now leading into the flop, which strongly hits her likely range of Broadway cards and Aces. I have no pair and only a marginal backdoor flush draw. Continuing here would be a waste of chips, especially with only a few hands left in the match. I will fold.

I’m on the button with Q♠3♣ and the pot is 150; to see the flop costs 100 (pot will be 250), so a call requires ~40% equity. Q3o is a weak, often-dominated hand and against two blind defenders my actual equity is well below that. Raising (min to 200 or larger) has little fold equity here — both Flash and Elon have shown they defend and 3‑bet fairly often — so a raise would frequently put me in a difficult spot out of value. With a healthy 64.5BB stack I’ll preserve chips for better spots rather than investing 100 with a negative‑EV hand. Fold.

In a blind-vs-blind scenario after the Button has folded, A8o is a strong enough hand to raise for value and protection. I will make a standard open-raise to 300 to put pressure on Elon in the Big Blind.

Facing a standard 3x open-raise from the small blind into an unopened pot after button fold, with Minni out. My Th9d is a standard big blind defending hand against the wide SB range (likely 40%+). It has good playability with straight potential, broadway cards, and solid equity (~42-45% vs typical SB range). Pot odds are excellent (calling 200 into 400 for 3:1). Folding is too tight, 3-betting offsuit not ideal without position or stronger blockers. Will call and use position post-flop to realize equity cautiously OOP vs aggressive Flash.

On a board of Ac-Td-Ts, I have top pair (Aces) with a weak kicker (Eight), and there's a pair of Tens on the board. This is a very strong board for my pre-flop raising range. Since I have the Ace of Spades and there's an Ace of Clubs on the board, it's unlikely Elon has an Ace. While a Ten is possible, it's more likely he has various underpairs or air. I will lead with a continuation bet of about half the pot to get value from weaker hands and protect my equity.

Flopped trip tens (Th pairs the Td/Ts on board) with a 9 kicker on a paired Ac Td Ts board. Monster hand, crushes Flash's SB opening range which includes Ax, KTs+, suited connectors, bluffs. His small c-bet 325 into 650 pre pot suggests weakness or standard continuation. Deep stacks (18k eff vs his 14k), position on him OOP. Raise for thin value against Ax/draws/worse Tx, protect vs draws (though few: A-high flush draws unlikely, straight draws minimal on paired board). Size to 1200 total (pot-committing lightly if shoved but sets up max value; ~3.7x his bet, standard vs aggro). Avoid flat OOP allowing free river or cheap turns.

On a board of Ac-Td-Ts, I have top pair with an eight kicker. While this is a strong hand, the board is highly coordinated. My continuation bet was met with a significant raise (nearly 4x) from Elon in the Big Blind. Elon's range for calling a small blind raise includes several Tens (JTs, QTs, KTs, 9Ts) which now have trips, as well as several Aces that outkick mine (AJ, AQ, AK). While it is possible he is bluffing on a scary board, the size of the raise suggests extreme strength or a very high-frequency bluff. Given that we are near the end of the session (Hand 146/150), I choose to avoid a high-variance, expensive spot where I am likely outkicked or facing trips.

In a 3-handed game, J9o is a standard opening hand from the button. I have a healthy stack and should look to apply pressure on the blinds, particularly the shorter stack in the Big Blind. Raising to 300 (3x BB) is a standard open to take control of the pot early.

Ts6c is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability, especially out of position in the small blind. Flash's button raise to 300 (3x) is standard and his range is wide but still favors better hands that dominate mine. Calling 250 OOP risks difficult postflop spots multiway with Minni acting behind, and raising is unprofitable with no strong blockers or equity. Pot odds are not compelling for speculative value. Standard fold to preserve stack in late position pressure.

8♦5♣ is a weak, unsuited, disconnected hand that plays poorly out of position. Flash opened to 300 from the button; the pot is 450 and I must call 200 to see the flop. Calling 200 into a 450 pot gives me a pot of 650 and requires ~30.8% equity to break even (200/650). Against a reasonable button open-range 8-5o is frequently dominated by higher cards and has well under that required equity, plus I’ll be OOP for the whole hand. 3-betting light to 500 would be a loose bluff that puts a large portion of my relatively shallowish 63BB stack at risk with a garbage hand. Preserve the stack and fold — wait for better spot or suited/connectors to defend in the big blind.

Preflop on the button with 8c3c, a speculative suited connector but low-ranked and weak overall. Pot is 150 (likely SB limp 50 + BB 100), to call 100 for ~2.2:1 if heads-up, but multiway risk. Position is excellent, stacks deep (mine 192bb, Flash 143bb, Minni 63bb). Opponents from history fold frequently to button aggression, especially Minni who limps here weakly from SB. Raising light to steal blinds/limp is +EV, applies fold equity, and if called, hand has straight/flush potential postflop. Folding trash is too passive late in game when maximizing stack; calling builds pot OOP poorly. Raise to 400 total (4x BB, standard isolation size in limped pot).

Call to 400 (I add 350 more). A7o is a defendable hand from the small blind vs a button open — it blocks some strong Ax combos and plays reasonably postflop with 63bb effective. Elon has been opening a wide, aggressive raising range (lots of 300–400 opens), and Flash in the big blind will sometimes fold or just call; I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal 3‑bet bluff. A 3‑bet to the min (700) with A7o is thin for value and won’t get enough folds to be a profitable bluff against two capable callers. Calling keeps the pot manageable, preserves stack depth for postflop maneuvering, and lets me play for value when I hit an Ace or selectively bluff on favorable runouts. If the BB squeezes big or Elon 4‑bets I’ll reassess (likely fold unless the sizing and reads justify continuing).

I am in the Big Blind with 6-5 offsuit. Elon (Button) has raised to 400 and Minni (Small Blind) has called. While the pot odds are roughly 3:1 to call (300 to win a pot of 900), 6-5 offsuit is a very weak hand to play multi-way and out of position, especially against the chip leader and a short-stacked opponent. Given that we are at hand 148 of 150, I want to preserve my stack and avoid marginal post-flop situations. Folding is the most disciplined and safe play here.

Flop is Kc Qc 2s. I have Ad7s — Ace‑high, no club draw (only a backdoor spade) and I’m out of position. Elon was the preflop raiser and checked the flop, which can be slowplay, pot control with K/Q, or a check with air. Leading out OOP with only Ace‑high here has limited equity and would commit chips into a pot against many continuing hands (Kx/Qx/pair+club draws) that can call and put me in tough spots on later streets. I prefer pot control and information — check back to see how Elon responds. Plan: if he checks back we get a free turn to realize equity; if he bets a small size I’ll call sometimes to keep the pot manageable and fold to big pressure; if he check‑raises or barrels large I’ll fold. Checking preserves stack and preserves fold equity for later when I hit an Ace or get a better runout.

Flop of 2s Qc Kc gives me a strong four-flush club flush draw (Kc Qc 8c 3c), approximately 9 outs (35-40% equity heads-up). Board is highly coordinated with broadway straight draws, flush draws, and pair potential. Minni checks from SB after calling a button raise OOP, suggesting possible weakness, a weak pair, or a weaker draw. With position and deep stack vs. shorter Minni (5950), this is a prime spot for a semi-bluff bet: value from worse made hands/draws (e.g., weaker flush draws, gutshots), fold equity against air or marginal holdings, and protection against her improving. Late in the session (hand 148/150), building the pot with strong equity while applying pressure maximizes EV. Bet 2/3 pot (600) balances value, bluff, and pot-building without overcommitting.

I’m OOP with Ad7s on Kc Qc 2s. Elon (button) is an aggressive, wide opener and a lot of his flop-betting range will include Kx, Qx, club draws and plenty of air. The pot is 1500 and his bet is 600; calling 600 to win a 2100 pot requires ~28.6% equity. A7o blocks some A‑combos, has decent showdown value versus a wide c‑betting range, and has enough equity to justify a call rather than folding. Raising as a bluff here is too thin — I have no club and I’m OOP, so I'd often face calls from club draws and pairs and be in bad shape on later streets. Calling keeps the pot manageable and preserves stack depth to apply pressure or fold on later signs of strength; if I see a safe turn (non‑club, non‑bricking card) I can continue or fold to heavy action, and if I pair my Ace I’ll pursue value. Call 600.

I check. I have A♦7♠ — Ace‑high only, no club backdoor and I’m out of position. Elon is a wide, aggressive BTN opener who c‑bet the K♣ Q♣ 2♠ flop; I called the flop to keep the pot manageable and realize equity. The 5♠ on the turn doesn’t improve me — it actually helps some of his ranges (extra spade and random paired undercards) and doesn’t change that I’m behind most of his value/club‑draw hands. Betting as a bluff OOP here is too thin: I’ll get called by club draws and K/Q hands and crushed by any turn improvement; I don’t have enough fold equity or hand equity to justify firing. Checking preserves stack, lets me see how Elon chooses to proceed, and keeps river options open (value if I pair an Ace, or a controlled bluff on a safe river). Plan: check the turn; if Elon checks back we see a free river; if he bets small I’ll consider a call to probe the river, but I will fold to heavy pressure/large raises given my lack of made hand.

Turn board 5s adds another spade, completing no draws for me but increasing coordination (spade flush draw now possible). I retain a strong 9-out club flush draw (~19% equity to river, 35% if called). Minni's check-check after calling flop c-bet OOP suggests capped range: likely weak Kx, Qx, suited broadway draws that missed, or bluffs that continue weak; less likely sets/TPTK given passive line. With position, deep stack vs her 5350 (2.5x pot), this is ideal semi-bluff spot: bet extracts value from worse draws/weak pairs, fold equity vs air/marginal hands (history shows Minni folds to turn aggression often, e.g., Hands 4,8,13+), protection vs her improving, and builds pot for river shove if she folds/showdown. Pot 2100, bet 2/3 pot (1400) sizes well: fold equity without pricing her off worse, leaves her ~3950 behind for river flexibility. Checking risks giving free card to her draws/completions and missing value; aggression maximizes EV late in session (148/150).

Elon barrels the turn for 1400 into ~3500 on K♣ Q♣ 2♠ 5♠. To call 1400 I need ~28.6% equity (1400 / (3500+1400)). I have A♦7♠ — essentially Ace‑high with only 6 clean outs to a pair (the remaining aces and sevens) → ~13% to improve, and my real equity versus Elon’s flop‑cbet/turn‑barrel range (Kx/Qx/club draws/overpairs and some bluffs) is well below the required threshold. Raising as a bluff OOP is too thin: I don’t block clubs meaningfully, he’s aggressive and will often call or shove, and I’d be committing a large portion of my stack on a marginal line. Calling leaves me in a very uncomfortable spot on the river with poor fold equity and limited improvement chances. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

Button with AdTc and 53.5BB effective — this is a clear open-raise spot. Pot is 150, 100 to call; I want to leverage position and fold equity while denying cheap flops to the blinds. I’ll open to 3x the BB (300 total) — a standard sizing here that’s consistent with my table image, keeps the pot manageable, and discourages speculative flats. Plan: c-bet favorable flops and fold to large, polarized 3-bets; call smaller 3-bets and play postflop in position.

I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Ten offsuit. Minni opened from the Button for 300. In a three-handed game, JTo is a strong enough hand to defend against a standard Button raise. Calling the 250 additional chips is a reasonable price to see a flop, though I will be out of position for the rest of the hand. I'll call and see how the Big Blind reacts.

Preflop in BB with A7s suited, facing button minraise to 300 and SB flat call. Pot 700, calling 200 more gives great odds (~3.5:1 direct, better implied). Button likely wide range, SB flat often weak speculative. A7s is strong defending hand: ace blocker, suited for nut potential, good playability multiway. However, best play is squeeze 3-bet to exploit: isolate button's wide range, fold out SB's marginal call, retain fold equity. Size to ~10bb total (1000) balanced for deep stacks, Minni's 50bb stack can call lighter but our hand crushes vs typical calling range. Postflop OOP advantage with initiative. Fold too tight, flat playable but 3bet higher EV.

I opened to 300 with AdTc and Elon has 3‑bet to 1000. With my stack ~5050 (≈50.5BB) this is a medium‑sized 3‑bet. Folding here surrenders position and too much equity; 4‑betting would polarize and commit chips with a hand that is often dominated. Calling 700 more (1000 total) gives me pot odds of ~30% (700/(1600+700) ≈ 30.4%) — AdTc has roughly that equity vs a balanced 3‑bet range and I keep the decisive advantage of position. If Flash (SB) folds after my call I can play heads‑up in position and c‑bet appropriate boards; if Flash calls and it becomes a 3‑way pot I’ll tighten up, control the pot on dry textures and only continue vs reasonable odds or good equity. Calling preserves flexibility and maximizes expected value here.

I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Ten offsuit. After I initially called a Button raise of 300, the Big Blind squeezed to 1000 and the Button called. Although the pot odds are roughly 3.3 to 1 to see the flop, JTo is a hand that plays very poorly in three-bet multi-way pots, particularly out of position. The Big Blind's range for squeezing here is typically quite strong, and I face significant reverse implied odds if I hit a pair and am outkicked by hands like AJ, KJ, or AT. Given this is the penultimate hand of the session, I will choose the disciplined fold to preserve my stack.

Preflop 3bet with A7o as a squeeze worked, Flash folded, Minni called with position. Flop 7c8h9d pairs my 7 for bottom pair top ace kicker, blocking strong Ax hands. Board connected and drawy (OESD 56/10J, flush backdoor), vulnerable to turns like 6/10/J/T. 2300 pot, effective stack Minni 4350 (SPR ~1.9), betting charges draws and worse pairs (86o+, worse A7, draws), represents strong 3bet range (sets, overpairs, str8 draws). C-bet 1/2 pot standard: value from floats/draws, protection, initiative OOP. Check risks free card or tough spot vs bet. Vs Minni's history of calling but folding post (e.g., recent hands), fold equity decent. Plan: bet 1200, call shove/call raise if small, fold to huge raise.

I have A♦T♣ on 8♥9♦7♣ after calling Elon's 3‑bet preflop. Elon leads for 1,200 into a 3,500 pot (a small ~34% pot bet). I have two overcards plus an open‑ended straight draw (T gives 6 or J to complete) — ~8 outs, ~31% to improve by the river. The call requires ~25.5% equity (1,200/(3,500+1,200)), so this is +EV. I’m in position so I can realize equity, apply pressure on favorable turns, and fold to extreme aggression if necessary. Raising as a semi‑bluff is an option but would polarize and commit a large fraction of my ~4,350 stack against a 3‑bettor who can shove or call with many hands; folding concedes too much equity and is too passive. Plan: call the 1,200, see the turn; if I pair or complete my straight I’ll extract value, if a safe card comes I’ll check‑control and re‑evaluate vs further aggression.

Turn Kc is somewhat scary (Kx improvements, club flush draw potential, possible 10J straight from flop), but Minni's range for calling 3bet pre and flop cbet OOP on 789 board is narrow: likely Ax (worse kicker), 10x/Jx draws/gutshots, mid pairs 88/99 (sets unlikely), floats. My 7hAs is bottom set blocker? No, bottom pair with TPK, blocks strong Ax and 87/97 etc. Beats worse pairs/draws/Ax, loses to 10J/88/99/K9/K8/K7. Her calling flop without raise suggests no nuts, consistent with history of passive calls then folds. Pot 4700, her 3150 (SPR 1), betting extracts max value from <1 pair/draws, denies equity. OOP check risks free river or her valuebet; bet initiative/value. Size 2400 (~1/2 pot) balances value/protection, sets up river nicely if check-raise. Near endgame, exploit her stack.

I’m on the button with A♦ T♣ facing Elon's turn bet to 2,400 into a 7,100 pot. I have an open‑ended straight draw (J or 6 = 8 clean outs) plus three remaining Aces (and three remaining tens as marginal outs), so my raw river equity is in the mid‑20s to low‑30s depending on which marginal outs I count. The call requires ~25.3% equity (2,400 / (7,100+2,400) ≈ 25.3%).

Calling is the best option: I keep position, realize my draw equity, and preserve fold options on the river while committing a manageable portion of my stack (I’d still have 750 behind if I call). Raising all‑in for an extra 750 buys little fold equity against a big‑stack 3‑bettor who will call light with many Kx and value hands; folding surrenders too much equity. 

Plan: call 2,400. If I hit a straight or pair of aces on the river I will extract value (shove or bet depending on the card and Elon’s action). If the river misses my draws and Elon shows heavy strength I can fold and preserve my short remainder.

River 6c completes club flushes and low straights (e.g., 10J for 678910 straight, or 56 for straight flush draw completion), but also bricks some draws. My A7h gives bottom pair top kicker (pair of 7s), blocking strong Ax. Minni called preflop 3bet OOP, flop cbet on drawy 789 board, and turn bet after Kc (scary card). Her range is capped: likely 88/99/OTTDs like 10J/56 clubs-suited that called down, weaker Ax, Kx, or bluffs. Beats floats/worse pairs/draw misses/A-high; loses to sets, 10J straight, club flushes (e.g., AcXc, KcXc). History shows Minni calls multi streets but folds to pressure (e.g., recent folds to river bets). Pot 9500, her 750 left (SPR ~0.08), shoving extracts max from worse (e.g., 88, Ax, missed draws) who call light, denies her bluff-shove or check-behind. OOP first, betting maintains initiative/rep; checking risks her value-shove (we call wide) or free showdown loss. Endgame hand 149/150, maximize stack: thin value shove standard vs calling station tendencies.

The 6 on the river completed my straight (6-7-8-9-10 with my T♣). On this board (8♥ 9♦ 7♣ K♣ 6♣) the only realistic hands that beat me are a club flush (villain holds two clubs) or a higher straight containing a J and a T. Elon’s line (preflop 3-bet, small flop bet, a bigger turn continuation, then a tiny 750 river bet into a 10,250 pot) looks like a blocking/thin‑value sizing or a small bluff — not a polarizing overbet representing a nut. Calling 750 into a 10,250 pot gives me ~6.8% required equity (750 / 11,000), which is extremely cheap. My made straight beats the bulk of his continuing range (Kx, one‑pair hands, missed broadway draws, many bluffs), so even accounting for the small chance of a flush or J‑high straight, a call is +EV. I commit and call the 750 (all‑in).

On the final hand of the game, I am on the button with Queen-Trey offsuit. This is a very weak starting hand that does not warrant an open-raise or a call against aggressive opponents like Elon and Minni. Since my goal is to maximize my stack and I am already up significantly from the starting point, there is no reason to risk chips with a sub-standard hand in the final hand of the session. Folding preserves my current stack of 14,000.

Jack-deuce offsuit is an extremely weak hand with poor postflop playability, no straight or flush potential worth noting, and dominated by most calling ranges. In small blind vs big blind, completing the 50 into 150 gives pot odds but commits to playing out of position against Minni's likely wide range while having equity disadvantage. Raising risks getting called or shoved on by better hands. Standard play is to fold this trash hand to preserve stack for the final hand, even though it's #150; discipline over desperation.
